\\u003cstrong\\u003eNative desktop app for macOS and Windows.\\u003c/strong\\u003e  ⚡ \\u003cstrong\\u003e100+ skills\\u003c/strong\\u003e · ✨ \\u003cstrong\\u003e150 brand-grade \\u003ccode\\u003eDESIGN.md\\u003c/code\\u003e systems\\u003c/strong\\u003e · 📦 \\u003cstrong\\u003e261 ready-to-use plugins.\\u003c/strong\\u003e  🖼️ Generates \\u003cstrong\\u003eweb · desktop · HTML / PDF / PPTX / MP4 export.  🤖 \\u003cstrong\\u003eRuns on Claude Code · OpenClaw · Codex · Cursor · OpenCode · Qwen · Copilot · Amp · Hermes · Kimi · Antigravity and 22 local CLIs\\u003c/strong\\u003e, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via BYOK.\\u003c/p\\u003e\\n\\u003cp dir=\\\"auto\\\"\\u003eOpen Design is what you get when the \\u003cstrong\\u003eagent-native\\u003c/strong\\u003e loop Anthropic shipped with Claude Design — discover the brief, lock the direction, stream the artifact, critique, deliver — stops being closed and becomes a \\u003cstrong\\u003efilesystem of skills, design systems, and plugins\\u003c/strong\\u003e that the coding agents already on your laptop can read, write, and remix. But it stayed closed-source, paid-only, cloud-only, locked to Anthropic's model, Anthropic's skills, Anthropic's surface. No checkout, no self-host, no Vercel deploy, no swap-in-your-own-agent.\\u003c/p\\u003e\\n\\u003c/blockquote\\u003e\\n\\u003cp dir=\\\"auto\\\"\\u003eOpen Design (OD) is the open-source alternative. Same loop, same artifact-first mental model, none of the lock-in:\\u003c/p\\u003e\\n\\u003cul dir=\\\"auto\\\"\\u003e\\n\\u003cli\\u003e🤖 \\u003cstrong\\u003eAgent-native, model-agnostic.\\u003c/strong\\u003e We don't ship an agent. The \\u003ccode\\u003eclaude\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003ecodex\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003ecursor-agent\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003ecopilot\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003ehermes\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003ekimi\\u003c/code\\u003e already on your \\u003ccode\\u003ePATH\\u003c/code\\u003e are the design engine. Swap with one click.\\u003c/li\\u003e\\n\\u003cli\\u003e🧠 \\u003cstrong\\u003eBrand-grade by default.\\u003c/strong\\u003e Every render reads the active \\u003ccode\\u003eDESIGN.md\\u003c/code\\u003e — a 9-section schema covering palette, type, spacing, motion, voice, anti-patterns. 150 systems ship with the repo (Linear, Stripe, Vercel, Airbnb, Apple, Tesla, Notion, Anthropic, Cursor, Supabase, Figma…). Drop a folder in, the picker finds it.\\u003c/li\\u003e\\n\\u003cli\\u003e🖥️ \\u003cstrong\\u003eLocal-first, BYOK at every layer.\\u003c/strong\\u003e Native desktop apps for macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel) and Windows (x64). Linux AppImage on the optional release lane. SQLite at \\u003ccode\\u003e.od/app.sqlite\\u003c/code\\u003e, files at \\u003ccode\\u003e.od/projects/\\u0026lt;id\\u0026gt;/\\u003c/code\\u003e, no telemetry, no cloud round-trip.\\u003c/li\\u003e\\n\\u003cli\\u003e🌍 \\u003cstrong\\u003eComposable on three planes.\\u003c/strong\\u003e \\u003cstrong\\u003ePlugins\\u003c/strong\\u003e carry runnable workflows · \\u003cstrong\\u003eskills\\u003c/strong\\u003e carry the agent's design taste · \\u003cstrong\\u003edesign systems\\u003c/strong\\u003e carry the brand. All three are plain files anyone can author, version, and publish.\\u003c/li\\u003e\\n\\u003cli\\u003e🔁 \\u003cstrong\\u003eRefresh an existing codebase.\\u003c/strong\\u003e Hand a \\u003ccode\\u003egit\\u003c/code\\u003e repo + \\u003ccode\\u003eDESIGN.md\\u003c/code\\u003e to the agent and it refactors your real components to the brand spec. Dedicated plugins migrate Figma / Pencil workflows into React / Next.js / Vue code.\\u003c/li\\u003e\\n\\u003cli\\u003e🔒 \\u003cstrong\\u003ePrivacy by conviction.\\u003c/strong\\u003e Everything runs where your data lives — your laptop, your team's server, your Vercel project. When the network is needed, the BYOK proxy is SSRF-guarded.\\u003c/li\\u003e\\n\\u003c/ul\\u003e\\n\\u003cdiv class=\\\"markdown-heading\\\" dir=\\\"auto\\\"\\u003e\\u003ch3 tabindex=\\\"-1\\\" Any MCP-compatible agent in another repo can read files from your local Open Design projects directly — tokens CSS, JSX components, entry HTML — as a structured API queryable by name. The agent always sees the live file, not a stale export.\\u003c/p\\u003e\\n\\u003cdiv class=\\\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\\\" dir=\\\"auto\\\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\\\"# One-line install (16+ CLIs supported):\\ncurl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh | sh -s \\u0026lt;agent\\u0026gt;\\n\\n# Then the agent can:\\nod search-files \\u0026quot;primary button\\u0026quot;      # MCP exposes the design source directly — the agent always sees the live file.\\u003c/p\\u003e\\n\\u003cp dir=\\\"auto\\\"\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003eFor an agent starting from scratch,\\u003c/strong\\u003e the installer places \\u003ccode\\u003e~/.config/\\u0026lt;agent\\u0026gt;/open-design.json\\u003c/code\\u003e (or the platform equivalent) plus a copy-paste MCP snippet. Cursor gets a one-click deeplink; Claude Code gets a \\u003ccode\\u003eclaude mcp add-json\\u003c/code\\u003e one-liner; every other agent gets JSON in the schema its config expects. Full per-agent flow → \\u003cstrong\\u003eSettings → MCP server\\u003c/strong\\u003e in the desktop app, or \\u003ca href=\\\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/agent-adapters.md\\\"\\u003e\\u003ccode\\u003edocs/agent-adapters.md\\u003c/code\\u003e\\u003c/a\\u003e.\\u003c/p\\u003e\\n\\u003cp dir=\\\"auto\\\"\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003eSecurity model.\\u003c/strong\\u003e Read-only by default, the daemon binds to \\u003ccode\\u003e127.0.0.1\\u003c/code\\u003e, and SSRF is blocked at the proxy edge. LAN exposure requires an explicit \\u003ccode\\u003eOD_BIND_HOST\\u003c/code\\u003e plus \\u003ccode\\u003eOD_ALLOWED_ORIGINS\\u003c/code\\u003e. Connector credentials and live-artifact preview routes stay loopback-only regardless.\\u003c/p\\u003e\\n\\u003chr\\u003e\\n\\u003cdiv class=\\\"markdown-heading\\\" dir=\\\"auto\\\"\\u003e\\u003ch2 a folder under \\u003ca href=\\\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/skills\\\"\\u003e\\u003ccode\\u003eskills/\\u003c/code\\u003e\\u003c/a\\u003e following the Claude Code \\u003ca \\u003ccode\\u003eod:\\u003c/code\\u003e frontmatter (\\u003ccode\\u003emode\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003eplatform\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003escenario\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003epreview.type\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003edesign_system.requires\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003edefault_for\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003efidelity\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003eexample_prompt\\u003c/code\\u003e). Drop a folder in, restart the daemon, it appears in the picker.\\u003c/p\\u003e\\n\\u003cp dir=\\\"auto\\\"\\u003eTwo \\u003cstrong\\u003emodes\\u003c/strong\\u003e anchor the catalog: \\u003ccode\\u003eprototype\\u003c/code\\u003e (web/mobile/desktop single-page artifacts) and \\u003ccode\\u003edeck\\u003c/code\\u003e (horizontal-swipe presentations). Also \\u003ccode\\u003eimage\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003evideo\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003eaudio\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003etemplate\\u003c/code\\u003e, \\u003ccode\\u003edesign-system\\u003c/code\\u003e, and \\u003ccode\\u003eutility\\u003c/code\\u003e modes. \\u003ccode\\u003eSKILL.md\\u003c/code\\u003e at minimum\\u003c/strong\\u003e; to list it in the Open Design marketplace, add an \\u003ccode\\u003eopen-design.json\\u003c/code\\u003e:\\u003c/p\\u003e\\n\\u003cdiv class=\\\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\\\" data-snippet-clipboard-copy-content=\\\"my-plugin/\\n├── SKILL.md            ← required: YAML frontmatter (name · description) + trigger phrasing + workflow (aim for \\u0026lt; 500 lines)\\n├── open-design.json    ← needed to list: marketplace metadata + inputs + pipeline + capabilities\\n├── README.md           ← optional: usage, install, registry links\\n├── preview/            ← optional: index.html / poster.png (strongly recommended for visual plugins)\\n└── examples/           ← optional: concrete use cases\\\"\\u003e\\u003cpre class=\\\"notranslate\\\"\\u003e\\u003ccode\\u003emy-plugin/\\n├── SKILL.md            ← required: YAML frontmatter (name · description) + trigger phrasing + workflow (aim for \\u0026lt; 500 lines)\\n├── open-design.json    ← needed to list: marketplace metadata + inputs + pipeline + capabilities\\n├── README.md           ← optional: usage, install, registry links\\n├── preview/            ← optional: index.html / poster.png (strongly recommended for visual plugins)\\n└── examples/           ← optional: concrete use cases\\n\\u003c/code\\u003e\\u003c/pre\\u003e\\u003c/div\\u003e\\n\\u003cp dir=\\\"auto\\\"\\u003eCore \\u003ccode\\u003eopen-design.json\\u003c/code\\u003e fields: \\u003ccode\\u003especVersion\\u003c/code\\u003e (currently \\u003ccode\\u003e1.0.0\\u003c/code\\u003e), \\u003ccode\\u003ename\\u003c/code\\u003e (stable ID), \\u003ccode\\u003eversion\\u003c/code\\u003e (semver), \\u003ccode\\u003ecompat.agentSkills[].path\\u003c/code\\u003e (points at \\u003ccode\\u003e./SKILL.md\\u003c/code\\u003e), \\u003ccode\\u003eod.kind\\u003c/code\\u003e (\\u003ccode\\u003eskill\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003escenario\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003eatom\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003ebundle\\u003c/code\\u003e), \\u003ccode\\u003eod.taskKind\\u003c/code\\u003e (\\u003ccode\\u003enew-generation\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003efigma-migration\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003ecode-migration\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003etune-collab\\u003c/code\\u003e), \\u003ccode\\u003eod.mode\\u003c/code\\u003e (the output surface, e.g. \\u003ccode\\u003eprototype\\u003c/code\\u003e / \\u003ccode\\u003edeck\\u003c/code\\u003e / data-discover=\"true\">.claude-plugin</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class=\"react-directory-row-commit-cell\"><div><div class=\"react-directory-commit-message\"><a data-pjax=\"true\" title=\"feat(daemon): per-agent MCP installer — plugin marketplace + 14-agent planner (#3442)\n\n* feat: add per-agent MCP installer with source-deploy fallback\n\nWire Open Design's stdio MCP server into 14 coding agents via a single\n`curl ... | sh -s &lt;agent&gt;` command. Two paths, chosen automatically:\n\n  Path 1 — a local `od` is found (PATH, packaged app, prior build):\n           register the agent via `od mcp install &lt;agent&gt;`.\n  Path 2 — no `od`: deploy from source (clone + pnpm install + build +\n           link + background daemon), then register.\n\n- od mcp install &lt;agent&gt;: per-agent planner with cli/json/manual\n  strategies; resolves the launch spec from the running daemon and\n  falls back to a default stdio spec when offline.\n- install-agent.sh: POSIX sh bootstrap that locates or source-deploys\n  `od` before forwarding; probes past the Unix octal-dump `od`.\n\n* feat(installer): support OD_REF to pin clone branch for source deploy\n\n* fix(installer): fail clearly when OD_HOME is a non-empty non-checkout dir\n\n* fix(installer): place server name before -e flags for claude mcp add\n\nclaude's `mcp add` treats `-e/--env` as variadic (`&lt;env...&gt;`), so emitting\n`-e KEY=val &lt;name&gt; --` made the CLI swallow the server name as an env value\n(&quot;Invalid environment variable format: open-design&quot;). Put the name first,\nthen env flags, then `--`, matching `claude mcp add &lt;name&gt; -e K=v -- &lt;cmd&gt;`.\n\n* feat(plugins): claude/codex one-command install via plugin marketplace; drop install.sh\n\nThe install.sh route was load-bearing on a non-existent URL: PR body\nadvertised `curl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh | sh -s &lt;agent&gt;`\nbut Vercel serves the Next.js index page at `/install.sh`, not the\nshell script. Bootstrap users got 173KB of HTML piped into sh and\nnothing installed.\n\nRe-scoped per maintainer call (lefarcen, 2026-06-01): drop the\nself-deploy path entirely, assume the user installs `od` themselves\n(brew / npm / DMG), and focus PR #3431 on the one-thing it\ngenuinely owns — wiring `od mcp` into the user's coding agent.\n\n## Two install paths now\n\n  Agent             User runs\n  ───────────────── ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n  claude            claude plugin marketplace add nexu-io/open-design\n                    claude plugin install open-design@nexu-io/open-design\n\n  codex             codex plugin marketplace add \\\n                       git@github.com:nexu-io/open-design.git\n                    codex plugin install open-design@nexu-io/open-design\n\n  cursor / copilot  od mcp install &lt;slug&gt;\n  cline / opencode  (writes the agent's own JSON config — see\n  openclaw / etc.    apps/daemon/src/mcp-agent-install.ts for the 14\n                     supported agents)\n\nBoth paths land on the same launch spec: `od mcp --daemon-url &lt;url&gt;`\nrunning as a stdio server, with whatever env the local daemon hands\nback from /api/mcp/install-info. The plugin manifest hard-codes\n`command: &quot;od&quot;` so it depends on `od` being on PATH — the maintainer's\nexisting install (brew/npm/DMG) is the source of truth, plugin install\nis purely the wiring.\n\n## What this commit does\n\n- Delete deploy/scripts/install-agent.sh (~240 lines) — load-bearing\n  but unreachable (Vercel doesn't serve it), and the source-deploy\n  path it gated (clone / pnpm install / build daemon / link bin) is\n  out of PR scope per the re-scope above.\n- Add .claude-plugin/marketplace.json — owner=nexu-io, exposes\n  open-design plugin at ./plugins/open-design.\n- Add plugins/open-design/.claude-plugin/plugin.json — name +\n  displayName + description + mcpServers pointer to .mcp.json.\n- Add plugins/open-design/.mcp.json — stdio server `od mcp\n  --daemon-url http://127.0.0.1:7456`, same shape /api/mcp/install-info\n  hands back.\n\nThe TypeScript planner + executor (apps/daemon/src/mcp-agent-install.ts,\nrunMcpInstall in cli.ts) and its tests are unchanged — they're the\ntrue core for the 10 agents that don't have plugin marketplaces.\n\n## Verified\n\n- JSON validates for all 3 manifests.\n- Daemon already serves stdio MCP on `od mcp` (pre-existing,\n  /api/mcp/install-info confirms).\n- `claude plugin install open-design@nexu-io/open-design` flow: I\n  validated the same manifest shape on a sister repo (nexu-xray,\n  https://github.com/nexu-io/agent-pr-explore) and it installs cleanly\n  on Claude Code + codex from a single marketplace manifest.\n\n## NOT verified yet (worth a smoke on this branch)\n\n- The full `od mcp install &lt;agent&gt;` flow for the 10 non-plugin\n  agents (cursor / copilot / cline / opencode / openclaw / antigravity\n  / gemini / trae / kimi / hermes-pi-vibe-print-only). The unit tests\n  cover plan generation; an end-to-end against a real `claude mcp\n  list` / a real cursor.mcp.json would catch shape regressions.\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: qiongyu1999 &lt;2694684348@qq.com&gt;\nCo-authored-by: lefarcen &lt;ontf116@gmail.com&gt;\" class=\"Link--secondary\" href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/commit/d285cb373ae4d1bade758e3e4b317fddaeb9fd03\">feat(daemon): per-agent MCP installer — plugin marketplace + 14-agent…</a></div></div></td><td><div class=\"react-directory-commit-age\"><relative-time tense=\"past\" datetime=\"2026-06-01T14:15:06.000Z\" class=\"react-directory-row-commit-cell\"><div><div class=\"react-directory-commit-message\"><a data-pjax=\"true\" title=\"feat: enable Vaunt contributor recognition with 5-tier system (#908)\n\n* feat: enable Vaunt contributor recognition with 5-tier system\n\nAdds .vaunt/config.yaml that maps the Open Design contributor scoring rules\n(spec §2.6) to Vaunt's achievement and point-action model. Once merged, the\nalready-installed Vaunt GitHub App will:\n\n- Backfill historical PR / review / issue / comment / discussion data for\n  the existing ~3.77k contributors silently (no comments are posted to old\n  threads — only achievements granted on Vaunt's own platform)\n- Calculate cumulative points per the rules in\n  open-design-bot/src/scoring.ts\n- Auto-grant the matching tier achievement (Spark / Signal / Node / Beacon /\n  Nova) once thresholds 0 / 30 / 150 / 700 / 2,500 are crossed\n\nTier icons (500x500 PNG, Twemoji on dark-space gradient) are checked in\nunder .vaunt/icons/ and referenced via raw.githubusercontent URLs.\n\nOnce Vaunt has scanned the repo we will use its API to populate\ndata/contributors.json + generate a CONTRIBUTORS.md leaderboard. No public\nvisibility for contributors until that step lands in a follow-up PR.\n\n* fix(vaunt): correct actor for review triggers + drop mis-mapped issue_resolved\n\nAddresses @mrcfps's review on #908 (two scoring/trigger mismatches that\nwould have produced incorrect backfill numbers).\n\n1. **Review actor mismatch.** Vaunt's documented actor matrix only allows\n   `actor: author` with `action: review`; `reviewers` is reserved for\n   `pull_request` triggers. With the previous config, no review event would\n   match, so reviewers earned 0 of the intended 3 review points, and the\n   Spark achievement's review entry-path was equally broken. Both review\n   triggers (point action `pr_review` + Spark achievement) now use\n   `actor: author`.\n\n2. **`issue_resolved` was wrong rule.** scoring.ts awards 6 points for\n   `discussion_answered_accepted`, not for closing an issue. The previous\n   `issue_resolved` action awarded 6 points to anyone who closed any issue,\n   which would have skewed both the historical backfill and future totals.\n   Removed `issue_resolved` entirely + removed it from every tier's sum().\n   Added an inline note explaining that `discussion_answered_accepted` is\n   not currently expressible in Vaunt (its discussion action exposes only\n   `closed`, no &quot;answered&quot; signal) and that the rule is moot today since\n   the repo has Discussions disabled — when both conditions change, we\n   re-add it.\n\nAlso added a header comment with Vaunt's actor/action matrix so future\nedits don't re-introduce the same kind of mismatch.\n\nCo-authored-by: Cursor &lt;cursoragent@cursor.com&gt;\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: ashleyashli &lt;ashleyashli@users.noreply.github.com&gt;\nCo-authored-by: Cursor &lt;cursoragent@cursor.com&gt;\" class=\"Link--secondary\" href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/commit/abe96caf062054d6952e8c2a0f44d67e802e1105\">feat: enable Vaunt contributor recognition with 5-tier system (</a><a class=\"issue-link js-issue-link\" data-error-text=\"Failed to load title\" data-id=\"4404947138\" data-permission-text=\"Title is private\" data-url=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/issues/908\" data-hovercard-type=\"pull_request\" data-hovercard-url=\"/nexu-io/open-design/pull/908/hovercard\" href=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/pull/908\" aria-keyshortcuts=\"Alt+ArrowUp\">#908</a><a data-pjax=\"true\" title=\"feat: enable Vaunt contributor recognition with 5-tier system (#908)\n\n* feat: enable Vaunt contributor recognition with 5-tier system\n\nAdds .vaunt/config.yaml that maps the Open Design contributor scoring rules\n(spec §2.6) to Vaunt's achievement and point-action model. Once merged, the\nalready-installed Vaunt GitHub App will:\n\n- Backfill historical PR / review / issue / comment / discussion data for\n  the existing ~3.77k contributors silently (no comments are posted to old\n  threads — only achievements granted on Vaunt's own platform)\n- Calculate cumulative points per the rules in\n  open-design-bot/src/scoring.ts\n- Auto-grant the matching tier achievement (Spark / Signal / Node / Beacon /\n  Nova) once thresholds 0 / 30 / 150 / 700 / 2,500 are crossed\n\nTier icons (500x500 PNG, Twemoji on dark-space gradient) are checked in\nunder .vaunt/icons/ and referenced via raw.githubusercontent URLs.\n\nOnce Vaunt has scanned the repo we will use its API to populate\ndata/contributors.json + generate a CONTRIBUTORS.md leaderboard. No public\nvisibility for contributors until that step lands in a follow-up PR.\n\n* fix(vaunt): correct actor for review triggers + drop mis-mapped issue_resolved\n\nAddresses @mrcfps's review on #908 (two scoring/trigger mismatches that\nwould have produced incorrect backfill numbers).\n\n1. **Review actor mismatch.** Vaunt's documented actor matrix only allows\n   `actor: author` with `action: review`; `reviewers` is reserved for\n   `pull_request` triggers. With the previous config, no review event would\n   match, so reviewers earned 0 of the intended 3 review points, and the\n   Spark achievement's review entry-path was equally broken. Both review\n   triggers (point action `pr_review` + Spark achievement) now use\n   `actor: author`.\n\n2. **`issue_resolved` was wrong rule.** scoring.ts awards 6 points for\n   `discussion_answered_accepted`, not for closing an issue. The previous\n   `issue_resolved` action awarded 6 points to anyone who closed any issue,\n   which would have skewed both the historical backfill and future totals.\n   Removed `issue_resolved` entirely + removed it from every tier's sum().\n   Added an inline note explaining that `discussion_answered_accepted` is\n   not currently expressible in Vaunt (its discussion action exposes only\n   `closed`, no &quot;answered&quot; signal) and that the rule is moot today since\n   the repo has Discussions disabled — when both conditions change, we\n   re-add it.\n\nAlso added a header comment with Vaunt's actor/action matrix so future\nedits don't re-introduce the same kind of mismatch.\n\nCo-authored-by: Cursor &lt;cursoragent@cursor.com&gt;\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: ashleyashli class=\"react-directory-truncate\"><a title=\"design-templates\" aria-label=\"design-templates, (Directory)\" class=\"Link--primary\" href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/tree/main/design-templates\" data-discover=\"true\">design-templates</a></div></div></div></div></td><td class=\"react-directory-row-commit-cell\"><div><div class=\"react-directory-commit-message\"><a data-pjax=\"true\" title=\"fix(landing): keep nav logo from shrinking in long-label locales (#3738)\n\n* fix(landing): keep nav logo from shrinking in long-label locales\n\nThe header brand is a direct flex child of `.nav-inner`, a\n`justify-content: space-between` row. With the default `flex-shrink: 1`,\nlocales whose nav labels are longer than English (de, ru, pt-BR, ...)\nwiden the link list past the container, so the browser shrinks every\nshrinkable child proportionally — including the brand. The single SVG\nwordmark then renders smaller (or collapses entirely) in those locales,\nwhich is why the top-left logo looked different sizes per language.\n\nPin `.brand` with `flex-shrink: 0` so the logo always holds its\nintrinsic 103x42; the nav links absorb the squeeze instead (they\nalready tighten and fold into the hamburger at the 1180/1080px\nbreakpoints).\n\n* fix(landing): mirror brand flex-shrink fix into landing template\n\nKeep the canonical open-design-landing template in lockstep with the\nshipped apps/landing-page CSS: add the same flex-shrink: 0 (and the\nrationale comment) to the template's .brand block in both styles.css\n(the regenerable source) and the baked example.html, so a future sync\ncan't silently drop the fix or show divergent preview behavior.\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: audit &lt;a@b.c&gt;\" class=\"Link--secondary\" href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/commit/2315cd309b63d3a6fe90f2f801ecbe90cb3df8c8\">fix(landing): keep nav logo from shrinking in long-label locales (</a><a class=\"issue-link js-issue-link\" data-error-text=\"Failed to load title\" data-id=\"4596009077\" data-permission-text=\"Title is private\" data-url=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/issues/3738\" data-hovercard-type=\"pull_request\" data-hovercard-url=\"/nexu-io/open-design/pull/3738/hovercard\" href=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/pull/3738\" aria-keyshortcuts=\"Alt+ArrowUp\">#3738</a><a data-pjax=\"true\" title=\"fix(landing): keep nav logo from shrinking in long-label locales (#3738)\n\n* fix(landing): keep nav logo from shrinking in long-label locales\n\nThe header brand is a direct flex child of `.nav-inner`, a\n`justify-content: space-between` row. With the default `flex-shrink: 1`,\nlocales whose nav labels are longer than English (de, ru, pt-BR, ...)\nwiden the link list past the container, so the browser shrinks every\nshrinkable child proportionally — including the brand. The single SVG\nwordmark then renders smaller (or collapses entirely) in those locales,\nwhich is why the top-left logo looked different sizes per language.\n\nPin `.brand` with `flex-shrink: 0` so the logo always holds its\nintrinsic 103x42; the nav links absorb the squeeze instead (they\nalready tighten and fold into the hamburger at the 1180/1080px\nbreakpoints).\n\n* fix(landing): mirror brand flex-shrink fix into landing template\n\nKeep the canonical open-design-landing template in lockstep with the\nshipped apps/landing-page CSS: add the same flex-shrink: 0 (and the\nrationale comment) to the template's .brand block in both styles.css\n(the regenerable source) and the baked example.html, so a future sync\ncan't silently drop the fix or show divergent preview behavior.\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: audit &lt;a@b.c&gt;\" class=\"Link--secondary\" href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/commit/2315cd309b63d3a6fe90f2f801ecbe90cb3df8c8\">)</a></div></div></td><td><div Code-compatible stream JSON format the daemon already parses,\nwith the prompt delivered over stdin. The daemon preserves AGENT_DEFS\norder in /api/agents and the web app fills an empty config with\nagents.find((a) =&gt; a.available), so listing Amp first made it the default\nengine ahead of AMR/Claude/Codex for any user with amp on PATH and no\npersisted agentId — inconsistent with its P2 classification. #4144) (#4147)\n\n* fix(release): pass RELEASE_COMMIT/RUN_ID to every publish step so manifests aren't stale\n\nFollow-up to #4144. Setting RELEASE_COMMIT/RELEASE_RUN_ID only on the\npublish-metadata step made publish-metadata.ts validateManifest() reject the\nper-platform manifests it aggregates:\n\n  refusing stale mac_arm64 platform manifest for 0.10.0.nightly.39: github.runId=0\n\nThe staleness guard compares each platform manifest's github.{runId,commit}\nagainst the publish step's currentRunId/currentCommit. The platform build jobs\nwrite those manifests but never received the RELEASE_* env, so they stamped\nrunId=0 while the metadata step now expected the real runId.\n\nMirror release-beta.yml: set RELEASE_BRANCH/REPOSITORY/RUN_ATTEMPT/RUN_ID/\nWORKFLOW workflow-wide so platform jobs inherit them, and pass RELEASE_COMMIT to\nevery publish step (4 platforms + metadata) so manifest github.commit matches.\n\n* fix(release): use resolved branch for RELEASE_BRANCH on every publish step\n\nAddress review: a workflow-wide RELEASE_BRANCH: ${{ github.ref_name }} stamps\nthe caller's ref under workflow_call / inputs.ref, so per-platform manifests\nwould disagree with the aggregate metadata (which uses needs.metadata.outputs.\nbranch). Drop the top-level github.ref_name and pass RELEASE_BRANCH:\n${{ needs.metadata.outputs.branch }} on each publish step instead, matching the\nresolved ref the metadata job built. Keep RUN_ID/RUN_ATTEMPT/REPOSITORY/WORKFLOW\nworkflow-wide (identical for caller and callee). class=\"react-directory-row-commit-cell\"><div><div class=\"react-directory-commit-message\"><a data-pjax=\"true\" title=\"refactor(chat): unify ask-user flow on &lt;question-form&gt;, delete AskUserQuestion mechanism (#4114)\n\n* refactor(chat): unify ask-user flow on &lt;question-form&gt;, delete AskUserQuestion mechanism\n\nThere were two parallel &quot;ask the user a clarifying question&quot; mechanisms:\n\n  A. The AskUserQuestion SDK tool — an inline interactive card in the chat\n     stream (AskUserQuestionCard / StreamingAskUserQuestionCard), whose answer\n     was fed back into the still-open stream-json child via\n     POST /api/runs/:id/tool-result + submitChatRunToolResult.\n  B. The &lt;question-form&gt; markdown artifact — a QuestionsBanner entry point in\n     chat, the form rendered in the right-hand Questions tab\n     (QuestionsPanel / QuestionFormView), answers returned as the next user\n     message.\n\nTwo visual languages for the same job, and mechanism A's card was visually\nbroken (the text status &quot;Awaiting your answer&quot; was stuffed into the 24px\n.op-status icon badge and overflowed onto the title). Mechanism A was pinned to\nmid-conversation clarification while B was pinned to turn-1 discovery — an\nartificial split.\n\nThis deletes mechanism A entirely and keeps only B, and widens &lt;question-form&gt;\nfrom &quot;turn-1 discovery only&quot; to ANY turn so mid-conversation clarification flows\nthrough the same banner → Questions tab → next user message loop.\n\nWeb:\n- Delete apps/web/src/runtime/ask-user-question.ts (parser trio).\n- ToolCard.tsx: drop the AUQ card/streaming-card/dispatch/imports and the\n  onAnswerToolUse / onSubmitForm props.\n- AssistantMessage.tsx: drop the live AUQ render path,\n  suppressAskUserQuestionFallbackText, the AUQ name from SNAPSHOT_TOOL_NAMES,\n  and the onSubmitForm bridge. Keep suppressDuplicateQuestionForms.\n- Delete the whole onSubmitForm / onAssistantFormSubmitStart return chain\n  across AssistantMessage / ChatPane / ProjectView / SideChatTab, and\n  submitChatRunToolResult in providers/daemon.ts.\n\nDaemon (conservative — keep the generic stream-json input skeleton):\n- applyClaudeStreamJsonRunBookkeeping: drop the AUQ detection branch and\n  run.pendingHostAnswers; stdin now closes on any non-tool_use clean terminal\n  turn.\n- Delete run-tool-results.ts, submitToolResultToRun, and the\n  POST /api/runs/:id/tool-result endpoint.\n- run-artifacts.ts:runAskedUserQuestion now scans streamed text for a\n  &lt;question-form&gt; marker (reassembled across text_delta chunks) instead of an\n  AUQ tool_use, preserving the run_finished.asked_user_question analytics\n  signal.\n- Keep --input-format stream-json / promptInputFormat: 'stream-json' as generic\n  mid-turn input infra; refresh the comments that referenced AUQ.\n\nSystem prompt / contracts:\n- Replace the Claude-only AskUserQuestion clarification section with generic\n  &quot;emit &lt;question-form&gt; for mid-conversation clarification&quot; guidance; relax the\n  API-mode override wording; drop &quot;Do not call AskUserQuestion&quot; from the\n  skip-discovery override.\n\ni18n / CSS:\n- Remove the tool.askQuestion* keys from types.ts + all locales.\n- Remove the op-ask-question* block and .op-status-awaiting from tools.css;\n  keep .op-status (the icon badge) and the qf-* classes that mechanism B uses.\n\nDocs / tests:\n- AGENTS.md: rewrite the runtime-conventions + chat-UI sections; add an\n  &quot;Asking the user questions&quot; section documenting the single mechanism.\n- Delete the AUQ-only tests; rewrite the quiet-period bookkeeping, run-artifacts,\n  prompt, and plugin-folder tests to the new behavior.\n\nValidation: pnpm guard (54), full typecheck, web tests, daemon suite\n(4351 pass), contracts tests; replayed two Claude stream-json traces through the\nmock CLI (incl. the [form answers — discovery] trace) — 0 parse failures, clean\nend_turn termination.\n\n* fix(chat): unify mid-conversation question-form across API/BYOK + analytics alias\n\nAddress review feedback on the ask-user unification:\n\n- contracts composer (used by API/BYOK 'plain' mode) now mirrors the\n  daemon-side '## Clarifying questions mid-conversation' section and widens\n  the API_MODE_OVERRIDE allowed-output list from 'discovery on turn 1' to\n  'discovery (turn 1) and mid-conversation clarification', so BYOK/API runs\n  route follow-up choices through the unified Questions tab instead of\n  drifting back to plain markdown option lists. Makes the AGENTS.md\n  'mirrored through packages/contracts/src/prompts/system.ts' claim true.\n- runAskedUserQuestion now matches the '&lt;ask-question&gt;' alias (already\n  whitelisted by the UI parser and daemon open-tag matcher), so a model that\n  drifts to the alias still records run_finished.asked_user_question instead\n  of being misclassified in the artifact funnel.\n- Tests: contracts api-mode asserts mid-conversation forms are permitted in\n  both daemon and plain mode; run-artifacts asserts the alias is detected.\n\n* fix(chat): make &lt;ask-question&gt; alias consistent across all paths + legacy AUQ renderer\n\nFollow-ups from re-review of the ask-user unification:\n\n- The &lt;ask-question&gt; alias is already accepted by the UI parser and the\n  daemon open-tag matcher, but two consumers still only recognized the\n  canonical &lt;question-form&gt;: db.listProjectsAwaitingInput's awaiting-input\n  filter and the next-turn transcript sanitizer. Both now match either tag,\n  so an alias-form turn (a) marks the project awaiting input and (b) gets\n  scrubbed from the prior assistant turn instead of replaying verbatim and\n  re-triggering the discovery-form loop.\n- Re-add a read-only legacy AskUserQuestion renderer in ToolCard. The\n  interactive mechanism is gone, but persisted AUQ tool_use events survive\n  in upgraded chat history; without this they fell through to GenericCard and\n  surfaced the raw {&quot;questions&quot;:[...]} JSON. The card now renders an inert\n  question summary (model-authored text, no new i18n keys, no submit path).\n\nTests: alias awaiting-input case (project-status), alias-form sanitizer\nregression (sse), and legacy AUQ render + unparseable fallback (tool-renderers).\n\n* fix(chat): correct asked_user_question detector + legacy AUQ answer rendering\n\nRe-review follow-ups:\n\n- runAskedUserQuestion read `data.text` for text_delta events, but the\n  persisted SSE shape carries the chunk on `delta` (packages/contracts/src/\n  sse/chat.ts), so production &lt;question-form&gt; runs appended nothing and\n  run_finished.asked_user_question was permanently false (the old tests passed\n  only because they built the wrong `text` field). Now reads `delta` (still\n  accepts `text` defensively).\n- It also matched a bare open-tag, so a run that merely shows the literal\n  markup in a generated doc / code sample / HTML artifact could be\n  misclassified as a clarification turn and excluded from the artifact funnel.\n  Now requires a renderable closed block via a shared detector.\n- Extracted the renderable-form detector + helpers out of server.ts into\n  apps/daemon/src/question-form-detect.ts so the missing-artifacts guard,\n  awaiting-input status, and run analytics share ONE check (the codebase\n  comment already anticipated promoting this once it had a third consumer).\n  The `&lt;ask-question&gt;` alias is covered by that shared matcher.\n- LegacyAskUserQuestionCard now parses the persisted answer from\n  result.content and surfaces it (&quot;question -&gt; answer&quot;) so two historical AUQ\n  runs with different answers no longer render identically.\n\nTests: run-artifacts now uses the real `delta` shape + renderable bodies, adds\na literal-markup negative case; tool-renderers asserts the persisted answer is\nsurfaced.\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: qiongyu1999 class=\"react-directory-row-commit-cell\"><div><div class=\"react-directory-commit-message\"><a data-pjax=\"true\" title=\"feat(runtimes): register AMR (vela) as an ACP stdio agent (#2355)\n\n* feat(runtimes): register AMR (vela) as an ACP stdio agent\n\nAMR is the vela CLI's ACP runtime mode. `vela agent run --runtime opencode`\nspeaks ACP JSON-RPC over stdio (see vela's\n`specs/current/runtime/manual-agent-run-openrouter.md`); per\n`docs/new-agent-runtime-acp.md` we expose it through the same `streamFormat:\n'acp-json-rpc'` transport that already powers Hermes, Devin, Kimi, etc.\n\nThe new `defs/amr.ts` is the entire wiring — `buildArgs` returns\n`['agent', 'run', '--runtime', 'opencode']`, `fetchModels` reuses\n`detectAcpModels`, and the fallback list seeds the OpenRouter ids vela's\ne2e baseline uses. `executables.ts`/`app-config.ts`/`metadata.ts` get the\nmatching `VELA_BIN`/`VELA_LINK_URL`/`VELA_RUNTIME_KEY`/`VELA_OPENCODE_BIN`\nallowlist + install/docs URLs, so users can configure the per-agent env in\nSettings without leaking into other adapters.\n\nCoverage: `tests/fixtures/fake-vela.mjs` is a minimal ACP stub that returns\nthe documented `initialize` / `session/new` / `session/set_model` /\n`session/prompt` shapes; `tests/amr-acp-integration.test.ts` spawns it via\n`child_process.spawn` and drives a full turn through `attachAcpSession` and\n`detectAcpModels`, so the ACP transport contract for AMR is end-to-end\nverified locally even before a real `vela` binary is installed.\n\nValidated:\n- pnpm guard\n- pnpm typecheck (all workspace projects)\n- pnpm --filter @open-design/daemon test (2881/2881)\n\nDeferred: real OpenRouter-backed turn through a built `vela` binary —\nthe runtime def needs no changes for that path, only `VELA_RUNTIME_KEY`\nand `VELA_LINK_URL` in env (or Settings).\n\n* fix(runtimes/amr): pin a concrete default model and bare openai ids\n\nEnd-to-end validation against a freshly-built `vela` (nexu-io/vela@main)\n+ OpenRouter surfaced two contract details the first AMR runtime def\ngot wrong:\n\n1. vela rejects `session/prompt` with `session/set_model must be called\n   before session/prompt`. attachAcpSession in apps/daemon/src/acp.ts\n   skips set_model whenever the picked model is the synthetic 'default'\n   id, so AMR's fallback list must NOT include DEFAULT_MODEL_OPTION. The\n   def now ships a concrete `gpt-5.4-mini` as both `fetchModels`'\n   default option and `fallbackModels[0]`, which makes attachAcpSession\n   always send a real `session/set_model` for AMR turns.\n\n2. `vela --runtime opencode` auto-prepends `openai/` to whatever modelId\n   it forwards to opencode's openai provider. With OpenRouter-style ids\n   like `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`, opencode receives the double-prefixed\n   `openai/openai/gpt-5.4-mini` and replies `ProviderModelNotFoundError`.\n   The new fallback list ships the bare ids opencode's openai registry\n   actually knows about (gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.4-fast, etc.).\n\nStub + tests:\n- tests/fixtures/fake-vela.mjs now enforces the set_model gate the same\n  way real vela does, so a regression that silently goes back to\n  model: 'default' would surface as a fatal error in tests instead of a\n  hidden production failure.\n- tests/amr-acp-integration.test.ts pins both contracts: no 'default' /\n  no 'openai/' prefix in fallbackModels, and a negative case that\n  asserts session/prompt fails when no model is set.\n\nAdds `apps/daemon/scripts/verify-amr-real-vela.mjs` — a small dev-time\nrunner that drives `attachAcpSession` against a real `vela` binary and\nprints the daemon's chat events, so future protocol drift can be checked\nagainst an actual OpenRouter call.\n\nVerified locally: `vela agent run --runtime opencode` + OpenRouter\nreturns the prompted string (&quot;AMR-E2E-PASS&quot;) through the full daemon\npipeline; daemon test suite stays 2883/2883.\n\n* fix(runtimes/amr): substitute concrete model when chat run sends 'default'\n\nA plugin-driven AMR run from the UI surfaced a real-world hole in the\nprior commit:\n\n  json-rpc id 3: session/set_model must be called before session/prompt\n\nThe Default-design-router plugin (and any caller that doesn't pin a\nreal model) sends `model: 'default'` straight through, which the AMR\nruntime def cannot accept — vela rejects `session/prompt` without\n`session/set_model` and attachAcpSession skips set_model whenever\nmodel === 'default'. Just leaving DEFAULT_MODEL_OPTION out of the\nadapter's `fallbackModels` is not enough: the chat-run handler in\nserver.ts still forwarded 'default' verbatim.\n\nThis adds `resolveModelForAgent(def, resolved, env?)` as the\nsingle source of truth for the substitution:\n\n  1. If the caller picked a real id, pass it through.\n  2. Else, if `def.defaultModelEnvVar` is set and the daemon process\n     env has a non-empty value for it, return that (operator escape\n     hatch — see below).\n  3. Else, if the def's `fallbackModels` does NOT contain a 'default'\n     id, return `fallbackModels[0].id`.\n  4. Else, return the original value (the historic shape — defs that\n     list 'default' themselves are untouched).\n\nAMR sets `defaultModelEnvVar: 'VELA_DEFAULT_MODEL'`, so when\nopencode's openai-provider registry deprecates `gpt-5.4-mini`\nupstream, an operator can swap the fallback id without a code change\nby exporting `VELA_DEFAULT_MODEL=gpt-5.5` before launching tools-dev\n/ od. Worth noting the env var must live in the daemon's `process.env`\n(Settings-UI per-agent env values only reach the spawned child, not\nthe daemon's resolver) — the new field's docblock spells this out.\n\nCoverage:\n- `tests/runtimes/resolve-model.test.ts` — 8 unit tests covering all\n  four resolver branches plus the env-override happy path / fallback /\n  ignore-when-user-picked-a-real-id case.\n- `pnpm --filter @open-design/daemon typecheck` clean.\n\n* chore(runtimes/amr): move AMR to the top of the base agent list\n\nSo `AMR (vela)` shows up first in the agent picker / status views,\nahead of claude / codex. Pure ordering change; no behavior delta.\n\n* feat(amr): Sign-in / Sign-out button on the AMR Settings card\n\nThe first half of the AMR work assumed the operator would set\nVELA_RUNTIME_KEY / VELA_LINK_URL on the daemon process and never\nsurfaced login state to users. This adds the missing UX so a fresh\ninstall can drive the full path from Settings:\n\n  - GET  /api/integrations/vela/status   reads ~/.vela/config.json\n    for the active profile and returns { loggedIn, profile, user }\n    (without leaking the runtime/control keys themselves).\n  - POST /api/integrations/vela/login    spawns `vela login` once\n    (409 if one is already in flight). The vela CLI opens the user's\n    browser to the device-authorization page itself — Open Design\n    only needs to kick the subprocess off.\n  - POST /api/integrations/vela/logout   removes ~/.vela/config.json\n    so the next status read returns logged-out.\n\n`AmrAgentCard` is a dedicated agent-card component for AMR because\nthe existing `&lt;button&gt;` row can't host an interactive sub-control\n(nested interactive elements). It polls /status after a login click\nuntil the daemon reports loggedIn=true (or 5 minutes elapse), and\nexposes a Sign-out action on hover. Other adapters (claude, codex,\nhermes, …) keep their existing `&lt;button&gt;` card.\n\ni18n: 8 new keys (settings.amrLogin / Logout / LoggingIn / etc.)\nadded to en + zh-CN. Other locales spread `en` and inherit the\nEnglish copy until translations land.\n\nCoverage:\n- `tests/integrations/vela.test.ts` pins the config.json reader\n  against a tmp HOME — including the negative case where a profile\n  has user info but no runtimeKey (still logged-out), and the\n  secret-leak guard (&quot;rt-secret-*&quot; must not appear in the projection\n  payload).\n- `tests/components/AmrAgentCard.test.tsx` covers all four UI\n  states (logged-out, logging-in, logged-in, logging-out) plus the\n  click-propagation invariant the divergent card was built to keep.\n\n`pnpm --filter @open-design/daemon test` 2901 / 2901 passing.\n`pnpm --filter @open-design/web test` 1719 / 1719 passing.\n`pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm guard` clean.\n\nDev script side-effects: `apps/daemon/scripts/verify-amr-real-vela.mjs`\nno longer requires both VELA_RUNTIME_KEY and VELA_LINK_URL — if\nVELA_PROFILE is set, the vela CLI is allowed to resolve credentials\nfrom `~/.vela/config.json`. Added the two AMR `.mjs` fixtures to\n`scripts/guard.ts` allowlist with the executable-fixture / dev-runner\nrationale.\n\n* fix(connection-test): substitute model for AMR before attachAcpSession\n\nThe chat-run path in server.ts already routes the requested model through\n`resolveModelForAgent` so AMR / vela (whose CLI demands an explicit\n`session/set_model` before `session/prompt`) gets the def's first\nconcrete fallback id when the chat run ships `model: 'default'`.\n`connectionTest.ts` was wiring `attachAcpSession({ ..., model: model ?? null })`\ndirectly, which made the Test Connection button on the AMR Settings\ncard deadlock with the same `session/set_model must be called before\nsession/prompt` error the chat-run path already handles — surfaced as a\npermanent &quot;Testing connection…&quot; spinner in the UI.\n\nReuse the same helper here so Test Connection mirrors chat-run behavior.\n\n* test(amr): three-layer end-to-end coverage for the AMR login + turn flow\n\nThe PR up to this point shipped runtime + UI code with unit-level Vitest\ncoverage. model\nerror, surface an inline nudge under the error pill linking to Open\nDesign's hosted AMR gateway (https://open-design.ai/amr). AMR's own runs are excluded from the\nnudge — they keep the dedicated sign-in / recharge affordances.\n\n* feat(web): rework failed-run AMR guidance into per-case error UI\n\nReplace the single inline nudge with a per-case failed-run experience\ndriven by the run's error code + agent:\n\n- The error card is now neutral gray (was red) and always carries a\n  retry button; it is driven by the persisted per-message error event so\n  it survives a reload.\n- Non-AMR agent hitting a model/auth/quota wall: a theme-color promotion\n  card under the error card offers &quot;switch to AMR &amp; retry&quot; — switches the\n  run to AMR, opens Settings on the AMR card, and auto-retries once the\n  account signs in (ProjectView polls vela login status, independent of\n  the Settings pill lifecycle, with success / 5-min-timeout / unmount\n  exits).\n- AMR agent unauthorized: clearer copy + an &quot;authorize &amp; retry&quot; button.\n- AMR agent out of balance: clearer copy + a &quot;top up&quot; button to the AMR\n  wallet, with manual retry.\n- Settings AMR card: when opened from the nudge, it scrolls into view and\n  pulses, and an authorize-button coachmark (a fake hand cursor that\n  rises in and dismisses on hover) points at the sign-in control when not\n  yet authorized.\n\nanalytics: surface_view (run_failed_toast) on the promotion card and\nui_click (go_amr) on its action are retained. i18n adds chat.amrCard.*\nand chat.amrError.* (en / zh-CN / zh-TW translated; other locales fall\nback to en) and drops the old chat.amrErrorGuidance keys.\n\n* fix(daemon): require status context for numeric service-failure codes\n\nPer review on #3083: the model-service classifier matched bare HTTP\nstatus numbers (`500`, `502`, `429`, `401`), so ordinary CLI output like\n`line 500`, `read 502 bytes`, or `exit code 401` could be misclassified\nas a provider outage / auth wall and wrongly surface the AMR nudge. Now\na status number only counts when it carries explicit context (`HTTP 500`,\n`status 503`, `code: 401`, `502 Bad Gateway`); textual provider phrases\n(overloaded, bad gateway, service unavailable, rate limit, …) are\nunchanged. Adds fixtures proving unrelated numeric output stays null.\n\n* fix(web): keep error pill for failed runs ChatPane's card doesn't cover\n\nPer review on #3083: the per-message gray error pill was suppressed for\nevery persisted error status event, but ChatPane only renders the\nreplacement top-level error card for `retryableAssistantMessage` (the\nlast failed assistant). So a failed turn that is no longer last (after a\nfollow-up) or an older failed run in history showed neither the pill nor\nthe card — its error detail vanished, undercutting reload/history\nsurvival. ChatPane now passes `errorCardOwnerId` (the assistant id whose\nerror the card represents); AssistantMessage suppresses only that one\npill and keeps rendering StatusPill for all other error events.\n\n* fix(daemon): don't treat a process exit code as an HTTP status\n\nFollow-up to review on #3083: the status-context helper accepted a bare\n`code` prefix, so `exit code 401` / `process exited with code 429` still\nmatched and got classified as AGENT_AUTH_REQUIRED / RATE_LIMITED (the\nvery `exit code 401` case the comment calls out as noise). `code` now\nonly counts when qualified (`status code` / `error code` / `response\ncode`) or punctuation-bound (`code: 401`); bare `exit code N` no longer\nmatches. Under that window the pill flips\nback to 'Signing in…' and can later surface the timeout/error path even\nthough the user already canceled, defeating the behavior promised in\nthe PR description.\n\nFix the listener instead of every dispatch site: in the\n`login-canceled` branch, after the local reset (stopPolling +\nsetPending(null) + clear refs), optimistically mark every subscribed\npill instance as not-in-flight (`setStatus((c) =&gt; c ? { ...c,\nloginInFlight: false } : c)`) and `return` — skip the\nrefresh-and-reconcile branch below entirely. The next explicit refresh\n(component mount, user interaction, or a `status-changed` event) will\npick up the daemon's confirmed state once the child has actually\nexited.\n\nAdd a focused regression test that holds `/api/integrations/vela/status`\nat `loginInFlight: true` even after a successful `/login/cancel`,\nasserting that the pill stays at the Canceled → Authorize sequence and\nnever bounces back to 'Signing in…'. This test fails on the pre-fix\nlistener and passes on the new behavior; existing\n'cancels an in-flight AMR sign-in…' and 'reconciles late AMR browser\ncompletion to Signed in after local cancel' tests continue to pass.\n\nAddresses review feedback on #3158 (chatgpt-codex-connector, nettee).\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: lefarcen &lt;935902669@qq.com&gt;\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: a1chzt &lt;chizblank@gmail.com&gt;\nCo-authored-by: Amy &lt;1184569493@qq.com&gt;\nCo-authored-by: Mason &lt;jinmeihong0201@gmail.com&gt;\nCo-authored-by: Caprika &lt;56862773+alchemistklk@users.noreply.github.com&gt;\nCo-authored-by: open-design-bot[bot] &lt;282769551+open-design-bot[bot]@users.noreply.github.com&gt;\" class=\"Link--secondary\" href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/commit/df8a0faff6c262e157e0b8ef8e3adda7b4c70e17\">feat(runtimes): register AMR (vela) as an ACP stdio agent (</a><a class=\"issue-link js-issue-link\" data-error-text=\"Failed to load title\" data-id=\"4483002071\" data-permission-text=\"Title is private\" data-url=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/issues/2355\" data-hovercard-type=\"pull_request\" data-hovercard-url=\"/nexu-io/open-design/pull/2355/hovercard\" href=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/pull/2355\" aria-keyshortcuts=\"Alt+ArrowUp\">#2355</a><a data-pjax=\"true\" title=\"feat(runtimes): register AMR (vela) as an ACP stdio agent (#2355)\n\n* feat(runtimes): register AMR (vela) as an ACP stdio agent\n\nAMR is the vela CLI's ACP runtime mode. `vela agent run --runtime opencode`\nspeaks ACP JSON-RPC over stdio (see vela's\n`specs/current/runtime/manual-agent-run-openrouter.md`); per\n`docs/new-agent-runtime-acp.md` we expose it through the same `streamFormat:\n'acp-json-rpc'` transport that already powers Hermes, Devin, Kimi, etc.\n\nThe new `defs/amr.ts` is the entire wiring — `buildArgs` returns\n`['agent', 'run', '--runtime', 'opencode']`, `fetchModels` reuses\n`detectAcpModels`, and the fallback list seeds the OpenRouter ids vela's\ne2e baseline uses. `executables.ts`/`app-config.ts`/`metadata.ts` get the\nmatching `VELA_BIN`/`VELA_LINK_URL`/`VELA_RUNTIME_KEY`/`VELA_OPENCODE_BIN`\nallowlist + install/docs URLs, so users can configure the per-agent env in\nSettings without leaking into other adapters.\n\nCoverage: `tests/fixtures/fake-vela.mjs` is a minimal ACP stub that returns\nthe documented `initialize` / `session/new` / `session/set_model` /\n`session/prompt` shapes; `tests/amr-acp-integration.test.ts` spawns it via\n`child_process.spawn` and drives a full turn through `attachAcpSession` and\n`detectAcpModels`, so the ACP transport contract for AMR is end-to-end\nverified locally even before a real `vela` binary is installed.\n\nValidated:\n- pnpm guard\n- pnpm typecheck (all workspace projects)\n- pnpm --filter @open-design/daemon test (2881/2881)\n\nDeferred: real OpenRouter-backed turn through a built `vela` binary —\nthe runtime def needs no changes for that path, only `VELA_RUNTIME_KEY`\nand `VELA_LINK_URL` in env (or Settings).\n\n* fix(runtimes/amr): pin a concrete default model and bare openai ids\n\nEnd-to-end validation against a freshly-built `vela` (nexu-io/vela@main)\n+ OpenRouter surfaced two contract details the first AMR runtime def\ngot wrong:\n\n1. vela rejects `session/prompt` with `session/set_model must be called\n   before session/prompt`. attachAcpSession in apps/daemon/src/acp.ts\n   skips set_model whenever the picked model is the synthetic 'default'\n   id, so AMR's fallback list must NOT include DEFAULT_MODEL_OPTION. The\n   def now ships a concrete `gpt-5.4-mini` as both `fetchModels`'\n   default option and `fallbackModels[0]`, which makes attachAcpSession\n   always send a real `session/set_model` for AMR turns.\n\n2. `vela --runtime opencode` auto-prepends `openai/` to whatever modelId\n   it forwards to opencode's openai provider. With OpenRouter-style ids\n   like `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`, opencode receives the double-prefixed\n   `openai/openai/gpt-5.4-mini` and replies `ProviderModelNotFoundError`.\n   The new fallback list ships the bare ids opencode's openai registry\n   actually knows about (gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-mini, gpt-5.4-fast, etc.).\n\nStub + tests:\n- tests/fixtures/fake-vela.mjs now enforces the set_model gate the same\n  way real vela does, so a regression that silently goes back to\n  model: 'default' would surface as a fatal error in tests instead of a\n  hidden production failure.\n- tests/amr-acp-integration.test.ts pins both contracts: no 'default' /\n  no 'openai/' prefix in fallbackModels, and a negative case that\n  asserts session/prompt fails when no model is set.\n\nAdds `apps/daemon/scripts/verify-amr-real-vela.mjs` — a small dev-time\nrunner that drives `attachAcpSession` against a real `vela` binary and\nprints the daemon's chat events, so future protocol drift can be checked\nagainst an actual OpenRouter call.\n\nVerified locally: `vela agent run --runtime opencode` + OpenRouter\nreturns the prompted string (&quot;AMR-E2E-PASS&quot;) through the full daemon\npipeline; daemon test suite stays 2883/2883.\n\n* fix(runtimes/amr): substitute concrete model when chat run sends 'default'\n\nA plugin-driven AMR run from the UI surfaced a real-world hole in the\nprior commit:\n\n  json-rpc id 3: session/set_model must be called before session/prompt\n\nThe Default-design-router plugin (and any caller that doesn't pin a\nreal model) sends `model: 'default'` straight through, which the AMR\nruntime def cannot accept — vela rejects `session/prompt` without\n`session/set_model` and attachAcpSession skips set_model whenever\nmodel === 'default'. Just leaving DEFAULT_MODEL_OPTION out of the\nadapter's `fallbackModels` is not enough: the chat-run handler in\nserver.ts still forwarded 'default' verbatim.\n\nThis adds `resolveModelForAgent(def, resolved, env?)` as the\nsingle source of truth for the substitution:\n\n  1. If the caller picked a real id, pass it through.\n  2. Else, if `def.defaultModelEnvVar` is set and the daemon process\n     env has a non-empty value for it, return that (operator escape\n     hatch — see below).\n  3. Else, if the def's `fallbackModels` does NOT contain a 'default'\n     id, return `fallbackModels[0].id`.\n  4. Else, return the original value (the historic shape — defs that\n     list 'default' themselves are untouched).\n\nAMR sets `defaultModelEnvVar: 'VELA_DEFAULT_MODEL'`, so when\nopencode's openai-provider registry deprecates `gpt-5.4-mini`\nupstream, an operator can swap the fallback id without a code change\nby exporting `VELA_DEFAULT_MODEL=gpt-5.5` before launching tools-dev\n/ od. Worth noting the env var must live in the daemon's `process.env`\n(Settings-UI per-agent env values only reach the spawned child, not\nthe daemon's resolver) — the new field's docblock spells this out.\n\nCoverage:\n- `tests/runtimes/resolve-model.test.ts` — 8 unit tests covering all\n  four resolver branches plus the env-override happy path / fallback /\n  ignore-when-user-picked-a-real-id case.\n- `pnpm --filter @open-design/daemon typecheck` clean.\n\n* chore(runtimes/amr): move AMR to the top of the base agent list\n\nSo `AMR (vela)` shows up first in the agent picker / status views,\nahead of claude / codex. Pure ordering change; no behavior delta.\n\n* feat(amr): Sign-in / Sign-out button on the AMR Settings card\n\nThe first half of the AMR work assumed the operator would set\nVELA_RUNTIME_KEY / VELA_LINK_URL on the daemon process and never\nsurfaced login state to users. This adds the missing UX so a fresh\ninstall can drive the full path from Settings:\n\n  - GET  /api/integrations/vela/status   reads ~/.vela/config.json\n    for the active profile and returns { loggedIn, profile, user }\n    (without leaking the runtime/control keys themselves).\n  - POST /api/integrations/vela/login    spawns `vela login` once\n    (409 if one is already in flight). The vela CLI opens the user's\n    browser to the device-authorization page itself — Open Design\n    only needs to kick the subprocess off.\n  - POST /api/integrations/vela/logout   removes ~/.vela/config.json\n    so the next status read returns logged-out.\n\n`AmrAgentCard` is a dedicated agent-card component for AMR because\nthe existing `&lt;button&gt;` row can't host an interactive sub-control\n(nested interactive elements). It polls /status after a login click\nuntil the daemon reports loggedIn=true (or 5 minutes elapse), and\nexposes a Sign-out action on hover. Added the two AMR `.mjs` fixtures to\n`scripts/guard.ts` allowlist with the executable-fixture / dev-runner\nrationale.\n\n* fix(connection-test): substitute model for AMR before attachAcpSession\n\nThe chat-run path in server.ts already routes the requested model through\n`resolveModelForAgent` so AMR / vela (whose CLI demands an explicit\n`session/set_model` before `session/prompt`) gets the def's first\nconcrete fallback id when the chat run ships `model: 'default'`.\n`connectionTest.ts` was wiring `attachAcpSession({ ..., model: model ?? null })`\ndirectly, which made the Test Connection button on the AMR Settings\ncard deadlock with the same `session/set_model must be called before\nsession/prompt` error the chat-run path already handles — surfaced as a\npermanent &quot;Testing connection…&quot; spinner in the UI.\n\nReuse the same helper here so Test Connection mirrors chat-run behavior.\n\n* test(amr): three-layer end-to-end coverage for the AMR login + turn flow\n\nThe PR up to this point shipped runtime + UI code with unit-level Vitest\ncoverage. This commit adds the cross-layer regression net the live demo\nrelied on:\n\n1. apps/daemon/tests/integrations/vela.routes.test.ts (HTTP, Vitest)\n   Spins up the real daemon Express app via `startServer({port:0,...})`,\n   persists `agentCliEnv.amr.VELA_BIN = &lt;fake&gt;` into app-config.json,\n   and exercises every /api/integrations/vela/* endpoint against the\n   extended fake-vela stub:\n     - status reads ~/.vela/config.json under various states\n     - login spawns the fake, waits for config.json to appear, returns\n       pid + startedAt + profile\n     - 409 already-running guard with the stub's delay knob\n     - logout removes the file (idempotent)\n     - secrets (runtimeKey / controlKey) never leak in the projection\n     - login → status round-trip flips loggedIn=false → true\n\n2. e2e/tests/amr/turn.test.ts (tools-dev orchestrated, Vitest)\n   Boots a namespaced daemon + web pair through `createSmokeSuite`,\n   inlines a self-contained fake `vela` binary that handles BOTH\n   `vela login` (writes ~/.vela/config.json) and\n   `vela agent run --runtime opencode` (ACP stdio with the\n   `session/set_model must precede session/prompt` gate the real binary\n   enforces), then drives a complete /api/runs lifecycle for\n   `agentId: 'amr', model: 'default'` and asserts the assistant message\n   captures the fake's streamed text. shows).\n\nfake-vela.mjs extensions:\n   - Handles `vela login` argv by writing\n     ~/.vela/config.json for the active VELA_PROFILE and exiting 0 —\n     mirrors real vela's on-disk side-effect  including the new 8-test integration suite.\n   - `cd e2e &amp;&amp; pnpm test tests/amr`: 1 / 1 passing.\n   - `cd e2e &amp;&amp; pnpm exec playwright test ui/amr-login-pill.test.ts`:\n     1 / 1 model\nerror, surface an inline nudge under the error pill linking to Open\nDesign's hosted AMR gateway (https://open-design.ai/amr). The nudge\nfires `surface_view` (element=run_failed_toast) on impression and\n`ui_click` (element=go_amr) on the link.\n\nAlso teach the daemon to classify CLI-agent auth/quota/upstream failures\n(Claude Code, codex, ...) into specific API error codes\n(AGENT_AUTH_REQUIRED / RATE_LIMITED / UPSTREAM_UNAVAILABLE) instead of\nthe generic AGENT_EXECUTION_FAILED, so both the error message and the\nnudge key off accurate codes. AMR's own runs are excluded from the\nnudge — they keep the dedicated sign-in / recharge affordances.\n\n* feat(web): rework failed-run AMR guidance into per-case error UI\n\nReplace the single inline nudge with a per-case failed-run experience\ndriven by the run's error code + agent:\n\n- The error card is now neutral gray (was red) and always carries a\n  retry button; it is driven by the persisted per-message error event so\n  it survives a reload.\n- Non-AMR agent hitting a model/auth/quota wall: a theme-color promotion\n  card under the error card offers &quot;switch to AMR &amp; retry&quot; — switches the\n  run to AMR, opens Settings on the AMR card, and auto-retries once the\n  account signs in (ProjectView polls vela login status, independent of\n  the Settings pill lifecycle, with success / 5-min-timeout / unmount\n  exits).\n- AMR agent unauthorized: clearer copy + an &quot;authorize &amp; retry&quot; button.\n- AMR agent out of balance: clearer copy + a &quot;top up&quot; button to the AMR\n  wallet, with manual retry.\n- Settings AMR card: when opened from the nudge, it scrolls into view and\n  pulses, and an authorize-button coachmark (a fake hand cursor that\n  rises in and dismisses on hover) points at the sign-in control when not\n  yet authorized.\n\nanalytics: surface_view (run_failed_toast) on the promotion card and\nui_click (go_amr) on its action are retained. i18n adds chat.amrCard.*\nand chat.amrError.* (en / zh-CN / zh-TW translated; other locales fall\nback to en) and drops the old chat.amrErrorGuidance keys.\n\n* fix(daemon): require status context for numeric service-failure codes\n\nPer review on #3083: the model-service classifier matched bare HTTP\nstatus numbers (`500`, `502`, `429`, `401`), so ordinary CLI output like\n`line 500`, `read 502 bytes`, or `exit code 401` could be misclassified\nas a provider outage / auth wall and wrongly surface the AMR nudge. Now\na status number only counts when it carries explicit context (`HTTP 500`,\n`status 503`, `code: 401`, `502 Bad Gateway`); textual provider phrases\n(overloaded, bad gateway, service unavailable, rate limit, …) are\nunchanged. Adds fixtures proving unrelated numeric output stays null.\n\n* fix(web): keep error pill for failed runs ChatPane's card doesn't cover\n\nPer review on #3083: the per-message gray error pill was suppressed for\nevery persisted error status event, but ChatPane only renders the\nreplacement top-level error card for `retryableAssistantMessage` (the\nlast failed assistant). So a failed turn that is no longer last (after a\nfollow-up) or an older failed run in history showed neither the pill nor\nthe card — its error detail vanished, undercutting reload/history\nsurvival. ChatPane now passes `errorCardOwnerId` (the assistant id whose\nerror the card represents); AssistantMessage suppresses only that one\npill and keeps rendering StatusPill for all other error events.\n\n* fix(daemon): don't treat a process exit code as an HTTP status\n\nFollow-up to review on #3083: the status-context helper accepted a bare\n`code` prefix, so `exit code 401` / `process exited with code 429` still\nmatched and got classified as AGENT_AUTH_REQUIRED / RATE_LIMITED (the\nvery `exit code 401` case the comment calls out as noise). `code` now\nonly counts when qualified (`status code` / `error code` / `response\ncode`) or punctuation-bound (`code: 401`); bare `exit code N` no longer\nmatches. Adds fixtures for exit-code lines returning null.\n\n* chore(web): translate AMR card / error keys for 16 remaining locales\n\nPR #3083 added 10 new `chat.amrCard.*` / `chat.amrError.*` keys but only\nprovided en/zh-CN/zh-TW translations; the other 16 locales fell back to\nEnglish. Each fix\nincludes / updates a focused test.\n\n- runtimes/executables.ts: `packagedVelaOpenCodeCompanionTree` now\n  verifies the inner `opencode` executable exists + is runnable, not\n  just the directory. This closes the false-positive availability path\n  that let `detectAgents()` surface AMR as available even when the\n  packaged companion was empty / partially copied (mrcfps, 4 threads).\n\n- runtimes/executables.ts: `resolveAmrOpenCodeExecutable` now prefers\n  the bundled `&lt;OD_RESOURCE_ROOT&gt;/bin/libexec/opencode/opencode` over a\n  stale `opencode` on the user's PATH, so packaged AMR builds can't be\n  hijacked by a global installation.\n\n- web/EntryShell.tsx: when the Local CLI scan returns an available\n  agent and the previously-selected agent is AMR, switch the selection\n  to the first available local agent so the runtime and persisted\n  agent agree before Continue.\n\n- server.ts (model-probe branch): for AMR, check `readVelaLoginStatus`\n  BEFORE rejecting on an empty live-model catalog — a signed-out user\n  was getting `AMR_MODEL_UNAVAILABLE` (&quot;choose a model&quot;) instead of\n  the correct `AMR_AUTH_REQUIRED` (sign-in affordance).\n\n- server.ts (default model fallback): if the user asked for the AMR\n  agent default and the cached id is no longer in the FRESH catalog,\n  fall back to `liveModels[0]` from the probe instead of rejecting the\n  run as `AMR_MODEL_UNAVAILABLE`.\n\n- integrations/vela.ts: route `vela login` through\n  `createCommandInvocation` so an npm/Node-style `vela.cmd` / `.bat`\n  shim on Windows gets the correct `cmd.exe /d /s /c …` wrapping with\n  verbatim args (matches `execAgentFile` / chat-run spawning).\n\n- tools/pack/src/linux.ts: in containerized Linux builds, bind-mount\n  the host directory of `OPEN_DESIGN_VELA_CLI_BIN` and rewrite the env\n  to the container-side path. The host path was being passed in as-is\n  even though the default container only mounts /project, /tools-pack\n  and cache/home — `copyOptionalVelaCliBinary` saw a missing path.\n\nDeferred (out of scope for this PR):\n- `od amr status/login/logout/cancel` CLI subcommands (AGENTS.md\n  UI/CLI dual-track rule, server.ts:5763) — sizable surface; tracked\n  for a separate focused PR.\n- Strict `--require-vela-cli` for Windows + mac-x64 beta builds:\n  prematurely blocked — `@powerformer/vela-cli` only publishes the\n  `darwin-arm64` platform binary today; adding the flag elsewhere\n  would fail the builds. Revisit once win/x64/linux binaries ship.\n\n* fix(amr): hoist sendAmrAccountFailure above the AMR catalog preflight (TDZ)\n\nThe new signed-out AMR branch in the catalog preflight at server.ts:10875\ncalls `sendAmrAccountFailure(...)` to emit AMR_AUTH_REQUIRED, but the\nconst declaration sat ~100 lines below at the outer function scope. Because\n`const` is TDZ-aware, that branch would have thrown `ReferenceError:\nCannot access 'sendAmrAccountFailure' before initialization` for the\nexact users it tries to help — defeating the original intent.\n\nHoist the helper to just above the AMR preflight block so it's available\nto every AMR code path in this function. Behavior elsewhere is unchanged.\n\nAlso rerun the daemon test suite: `launch.test.ts &gt; resolveAgentLaunch\nuses packaged built-in Vela for AMR` was creating the\n`&lt;resourceRoot&gt;/bin/libexec/opencode/` companion *directory* only, but\nthis PR's earlier tightening of `packagedVelaOpenCodeCompanionTree`\nalso requires the inner `opencode` executable. Add it to that fixture\nto match the new contract; the test was a sibling of the executables /\nenv-and-detection fixtures already updated in 13fc4f4.\n\nAddresses #2355 review (mrcfps, 2026-05-28).\n\n* feat(web): add hover cancel for AMR login (#3158)\n\n* feat(web): add hover cancel for AMR login\n\n* fix(web): don't bounce AmrLoginPill back to 'Signing in…' after local cancel\n\nBoth codex-connector (P2) and looper (CHANGES_REQUESTED) on this PR\nflagged the same race in the new local-cancel path: `handleCancelLogin`\ndispatches `notifyAmrLoginStatusChanged('login-canceled')` immediately\nafter `/login/cancel` returns, but the `AMR_LOGIN_STATUS_EVENT` listener\nunconditionally re-enters `refresh()` and then restarts polling\nwhenever `/api/integrations/vela/status` still reports\n`loginInFlight: true`.\n\nThat is a real race because the daemon's `cancelVelaLogin()` only sends\nSIGTERM (escalating to SIGKILL after `LOGIN_CANCEL_KILL_GRACE_MS` =\n2000 ms) and keeps the child in `activeLoginProcs` until it actually\nexits — so the first `/status` read after a successful cancel can\nlegally still come back as in-flight. Under that window the pill flips\nback to 'Signing in…' and can later surface the timeout/error path even\nthough the user already canceled, defeating the behavior promised in\nthe PR description.\n\nFix the listener instead of every dispatch site: in the\n`login-canceled` branch, after the local reset (stopPolling +\nsetPending(null) + clear refs), optimistically mark every subscribed\npill instance as not-in-flight (`setStatus((c) =&gt; c ? { ...c,\nloginInFlight: false } : c)`) and `return` — skip the\nrefresh-and-reconcile branch below entirely. The next explicit refresh\n(component mount, user interaction, or a `status-changed` event) will\npick up the daemon's confirmed state once the child has actually\nexited.\n\nAdd a focused regression test that holds `/api/integrations/vela/status`\nat `loginInFlight: true` even after a successful `/login/cancel`,\nasserting that the pill stays at the Canceled → Authorize sequence and\nnever bounces back to 'Signing in…'. This test fails on the pre-fix\nlistener and passes on the new behavior; existing\n'cancels an in-flight AMR sign-in…' and 'reconciles late AMR browser\ncompletion to Signed in after local cancel' tests continue to pass.\n\nAddresses review feedback on #3158 (chatgpt-codex-connector, nettee).\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: lefarcen &lt;935902669@qq.com&gt;\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: class=\"react-directory-commit-message\"><a data-pjax=\"true\" title=\"chore: back-merge release/v0.10.0 into main (reconcile QA fixes; retiring release branch) (#3715)\n\n* chore(release): prepare 0.10.0\n\n* [codex] Add same-run retry runtime (#3576)\n\n* Add same-run retry runtime\n\n* fix(daemon): route retried-start and watchdog stall through retry finalizer\n\nTwo retryable failure paths bypassed the same-run retry finalizer, so they\nemitted no run_retry_attempted/run_retry_finished telemetry and could report\nthe fallback retry_final_result: 'not_attempted'.\n\n- restartSameRunAfterRetry's catch now routes a failed retried start through\n  finishWithRetryDecision instead of design.runs.finish, so it emits terminal\n  retry telemetry and sets run.retryFinalResult. retryAttemptCount is already\n  1 there, so decideSafeRunRetry suppresses with attempt_limit_reached and\n  cannot loop.\n- failForInactivity's no-output stall now routes through the finalizer after\n  surfacing stallPayload. A silent first-token hang classifies as a retryable\n  timeout at first_token_wait, so the same-run retry now recovers it. A new\n  watchdogRetryRestarted guard makes the SIGTERM'd stalled child's close bail\n  early (revoking only its own tool token) so it cannot re-finalize the run or\n  delete the new attempt's event sink (both keyed by the shared runId).\n- finishWithRetryDecision returns whether it restarted so the watchdog can set\n  the guard.\n\nAdds a run-retry-runtime test with a low OD_CHAT_RUN_INACTIVITY_TIMEOUT_MS\nproving the same-run retry fires for a silent first attempt.\n\n* fix(web): avoid duplicate style picker group keys\n\n* Fix Design Files and sidebar page layout regressions (#3629)\n\nFixes two web UI layout regressions:\n\n- Prevent Design Files name cells from collapsing to near-zero width, which caused generated file and folder names to wrap vertically while leaving empty space to the right.\n- Hide inactive entry views with display: none so Plugins, Integrations, Design Systems, and Automations no longer reserve hidden Home view height above their page content.\n\nValidated with web typecheck, focused web component tests, pnpm guard, and local visual checks.\n\n* fix(web): copy run error diagnostics (#3630)\n\n* fix(web): route project file The HomeHero\ncomposer gains a folder pill (pick / clear); the chosen path is threaded\nthrough project creation and applied via the working-dir API once the\nproject id exists.\n\nOn desktop the working-dir POST is gated behind a host-minted token, so\nadd a `dialog:pick-working-dir` IPC handler that shows the native picker\nand mints a single-use token bound to the folder, plus a\n`pickHostWorkingDir()` bridge + types/normalizer in `@open-design/host`.\nThe renderer feature-detects the bridge and falls back to the web folder\ndialog (no token) otherwise. Native dialogs now allow creating a new\nfolder inline (`createDirectory` / `ShowNewFolderButton`), and macOS uses\n`choose folder` so the panel reliably takes key focus.\n\nWire the WorkingDirPill into the project composer footer and surface a\nreloading state + breadcrumb root label to the Design Files panel while a\nreplace reindexes. Surface a create-time error toast when the working-dir\nhandoff fails (expired ~60s token or daemon rejection) instead of\nsilently leaving the project in the managed root while the user believes\ntheir folder was applied.\n\n* fix(web): abort create on failed working-dir handoff and always show root crumb\n\nWhen the pre-create working-dir handoff fails, short-circuit the rest of the\nHome submit path (skip the staged-attachment upload and auto-send) so the first\nrun cannot proceed against the managed `.od/projects/&lt;id&gt;` tree the user did not\nchoose, and reword the error toast accordingly.
