Removes extractJsonStringField.\n- Strip a (possibly partial) trailing ``` fence so the live preview keeps\n  streaming in the gap between the fenced JSON body closing and the\n  &lt;/question-form&gt; close tag arriving, instead of dropping back to empty.\n\nBoth covered by regression tests in question-form.test.ts.\n\n* fix(web): preserve submitLabel + string-safe fence trim in streaming form parser\n\nSecond round of review follow-ups on parsePartialQuestionForm:\n\n- Carry top-level submitLabel through the streaming preview, mirroring\n  tryParseForm, so a custom CTA label doesn't flicker in only once the\n  close tag arrives.\n- Only strip the trailing ``` fence when those backticks are the closing\n  wrapper, not content of a JSON string value still being typed — a label\n  like &quot;Use ```&quot; mid-stream kept losing its backticks. The trim now checks\n  it isn't inside an open JSON string first.\n\nBoth covered by regression tests in question-form.test.ts.\n\n* fix(web): honest status badge on WebFetch/WebSearch cards\n\nWebFetchCard / WebSearchCard hard-coded a green check badge regardless of\nthe real tool state, so a slow or failed fetch/search rendered as\nfalse-green. Thread result / runStreaming / runSucceeded through both and\nreuse ResultBadge (as the other tool cards do) so the badge reflects the\nactual outcome.\n\nPre-existing on main (only surfaced in this PR's changed range because the\nnew streaming card additions shifted these lines down); folded in here\nsince it's a one-line-per-card fix in a file this PR already touches.\n\n* fix(web): keep partial-JSON repair stable across a dangling string escape\n\nrepairJsonPrefix() appended a closing quote even when the stream stopped on\nan incomplete escape inside an open string, leaving invalid JSON: `{&quot;a&quot;:&quot;x\\`\nbecame `{&quot;a&quot;:&quot;x\\&quot;` (the quote got escaped) and `{&quot;a&quot;:&quot;x\\u` became an invalid\n`\\uXXXX`. parsePartialJson() then returned null, collapsing the live\nAskUserQuestion / question-form preview to its empty frame mid-stream\nwhenever a prompt mentioned a Windows path, regex, or escaped quote.\n\nNow neutralize a trailing incomplete escape before closing the string: drop a\npartial `\\uXXXX` (0-3 hex) and a lone (odd-run) trailing backslash. Completed\nescapes and even backslash pairs (e.g. `C:\\\\Users\\\\`) are preserved.\n\nRegression tests in ask-user-question.test.ts cover the repair cases and a\npath-like prompt that keeps its last visible content.\n\n* fix(web): trim unfinished scalar values in partial-JSON repair\n\nrepairJsonPrefix() only trimmed dangling commas / empty `&quot;key&quot;:`, so a delta\nthat stopped mid-scalar left invalid JSON: `{&quot;…&quot;,&quot;multiSelect&quot;:f` closed to\n`{…:f}` and failed to parse, collapsing the live preview whenever a boolean,\nnull, or number token split across deltas. The cleanup loop now also trims a\npartial `true`/`false`/`null` and a number cut on `.`/`e`/sign, leaving the\n`&quot;key&quot;:` / `,` / `[` boundary for the next iteration. Complete literals\n(`true`, `12`, `1.5`, `1e3`) and string values that merely start like a\nliteral are not prefixes of these patterns, so they survive.\n\nRegressions added in both the AUQ and question-form suites (a boolean split\nacross deltas keeps already-visible questions) plus repair-level unit tests.\n\n* fix(web): stable preview question ids while a form streams\n\nshapeStreamingQuestions surfaced a question as soon as its label existed, but\nmapRawQuestion falls back to q${index+1} until the real id streams — so a\nquestion that arrives label-first and gains its canonical id later would\nchange id mid-stream. The Questions panel stays editable while building and\nkeys fields / answers off q.id, so that id change remounts the field and\norphans whatever the user already picked. Pin the preview id to the array\nindex for the whole streaming phase (questions only append once their label\nexists, so the index is stable); the final non-preview parse still uses the\nreal id.\n\nRegression in question-form.test.ts: label-first then id-later keeps id q1.\n\n* fix(web): preview question ids match the final parse (no orphaned answers)\n\nThe previous index-pin kept ids stable *within* the preview but still\ndiffered from the final canonical id, so an answer entered while the panel\nwas building got stranded at the preview→final swap (field re-keys from `q1`\nto `platform`, reads nothing). Surface a streaming question only once its\ncanonical id is determinable instead: use the streamed `id`; or, for an\nalready-closed (not in-flight) object with no id, the `q${index+1}` fallback\nthat the final parse will also assign; hold the in-flight object until it\ngains an id. Preview id now always equals the final id.\n\nUpdated/added regressions in question-form.test.ts: a label-first object is\nheld until its id is determinable, and preview ids match splitOnQuestionForms.\n\n* fix(web): stable positional keys in the streaming AskUserQuestion card\n\nThe streaming card keyed fields on q.question and options on opt.label, but\nthose strings grow token-by-token, so every delta changed the key and\nremounted the subtree — replaying the reveal animation on each token instead\nof mounting once. Switch to positional keys (questionIndex /\nquestionIndex:optionIndex); questions and options only append during\nstreaming, so the index is stable and each node updates its text in place.\n\nRegression: StreamingAskUserQuestionCard.test.tsx asserts the field/option\nDOM nodes are reused (===) across successive grown prefixes.\n\n* fix(web): stream a closed no-id question before the close tag\n\nThe previous gate held *every* last no-id question as if in-flight, but a\nlast object whose braces have already streamed has a final fallback id\n(q${index+1}) and should appear immediately — holding it broke the\n&quot;question shows the moment its label exists&quot; behavior for forms that omit\nexplicit ids. Distinguish closed vs still-open via a string-aware\nbalanced-object count: surface a question once its object has closed (id\nfinal) or it already carries an `id` (stable); keep holding only an\nin-flight object with no id yet.\n\nRegressions: a single closed no-id question streams pre-close-tag (id q1),\nwhile the same content with an unclosed object stays held.\n\n* fix(web): suppress hedging fallback text while a live AskUserQuestion streams\n\nsuppressAskUserQuestionFallbackText ran on the persisted blocks before the\nliveBlocks pass, so during streaming (no persisted tool_use yet) seenAskUser\nQuestion never flipped and any duplicate hedging markdown survived into\npersistedBlocks while the live card was appended after — the exact\nduplicate-question state the card removes, just earlier in the turn. Seed the\nsuppressor with whether a live AskUserQuestion is present in liveToolInput so\nit fires during the streaming phase too.\n\nRegression in AssistantMessage.test.tsx: fallback prose coexisting with a live\nAskUserQuestion in liveToolInput is suppressed, while normal prose without a\nlive AUQ is kept.\n\n* fix(web): order-aware fallback suppression for live AskUserQuestion\n\nThe previous fix seeded the suppressor with &quot;a live AskUserQuestion exists&quot;,\nwhich dropped ALL text blocks in the turn — including legitimate preamble\n(&quot;I need one clarification:&quot;) that precedes the tool call, not just duplicate\nhedging. Make it order-aware instead: suppress text only after an\nAskUserQuestion in block order, and have the suppressor recognize a live\n(streaming) AskUserQuestion live-tool block the same way it recognizes a\nsettled tool-group. Preamble before the card is kept; hedging after it is\ndropped — matching the settled-case semantics.\n\nRegression updated in AssistantMessage.test.tsx: intro preamble coexisting\nwith a live AskUserQuestion in liveToolInput is preserved.\n\n* fix(web): place the live AskUserQuestion at its real stream position\n\nThe order-aware pass still appended the live AUQ block after all persisted\nblocks, so hedging text the model emits *after* starting the tool call (which\nis already in events, hence before the appended block) was kept. Carry the\nevent index the tool started at (`seq`, recorded in liveToolInput when the\nfirst input_json_delta arrives) and split the events around it: blocks before\n`seq` are preamble, the live AUQ block goes next, blocks after `seq` are\nhedging. Suppression then keeps the preamble and drops the trailing hedging,\nexactly matching the settled tool_use case.\n\nRegression in AssistantMessage.test.tsx: with the tool starting after the\npreamble (seq=1), preamble before the card is preserved and hedging after it\nis dropped.\n\n* fix(web): account for buffered preamble when pinning live-tool seq\n\n`seq` was read from `latestAssistantMsg.events.length`, but preamble text goes\nthrough `appendTextEvent` and isn't committed to `events` until the next\nrAF/250ms flush. When intro prose and the first `tool_input_delta` arrive in\nthe same burst, that read undercounts by one, so the live AskUserQuestion was\npositioned before the preamble and suppression dropped it as hedging. The\nbuffer now exposes `hasPendingText()`, and seq adds 1 for a still-pending\npreamble chunk (it commits as one text event just before the tool position).\n\nRegression in buffered-text-pending.test.tsx: appended text is reported\npending and absent from events until flush, then commits as one event.\n\n* fix(web): re-render the streaming row on live tool-input deltas\n\nAssistantMessage is memoized with a prop allowlist that omitted liveToolInput,\nso a burst of pure tool_input_delta traffic (which only changes liveToolInput,\nnot message) was swallowed by the comparator — the live AskUserQuestion card /\ncode box wouldn't grow until some unrelated change, usually the final\ntool_use. Add liveToolInput to ASSISTANT_MESSAGE_COMPARED_PROPS, and have\nChatPane pass it only to the streaming row (undefined elsewhere) so just that\nrow re-renders per delta, not all N messages.\n\nAlso widens ChatPane's liveToolInput type with the optional seq field.\n\nRegression in AssistantMessage.test.tsx: with the same message object,\nchanging only liveToolInput re-renders and grows the card prompt.\n\n(cherry picked from commit 6e10fa4852a2edf2ba1d25786fbc40e1c7de6c2b)\n\n* fix(web): keep the streaming question-form id stable (no churn)\n\n`parsePartialQuestionForm` derived the form `id` from the streaming body\n`id` token, but that arrives char-by-char and `parsePartialJson` repairs the\nopen string, so `form.id` churned (`&quot;d&quot;` → `&quot;di&quot;` → …). `id` keys the live,\nstill-editable Questions panel (ProjectView's questionFormKey, QuestionForm's\ndata-form-id / input names), so each partial-id change remounted the panel and\ndiscarded in-progress answers. Derive the preview id from the open-tag attr\n(complete the instant the tag streams) or the stable default instead; the\nfinal parse (tryParseForm, after the close tag) still adopts the real body id.\nThis also keeps the earlier nested-id-masquerade guarantee (no body scan).\n\nFollow-up to #3582. Regression: the form id stays `discovery` while the body\nid token streams in.\n\n(cherry picked from commit 353e4a64acb169a539308cdc0f87ba8a48d36c70)\n\n* fix(web): adopt the question-form body id once complete (no preview→final remount)\n\nBuilding on the char-churn fix: when a form omits the open-tag id attribute\nbut carries a body id, the preview used 'discovery' and the final parse\nadopted the body id — a single remount at the close tag that could drop\nanswers. Adopt the body id as soon as its string literal is terminated\n(depth-1, nested ids ignored); that value equals what tryParseForm assigns,\nso preview id == final id and the panel never remounts on the swap. The id\nsettles early (it's a leading field), before any question is answerable.\n\nRegressions: a partial body id is not adopted (no churn); a complete body id\nis adopted and matches splitOnQuestionForms' final id; nested question ids are\nignored.\n\n(cherry picked from commit 9576f98f561eb4c1ed7f35413575c418d55e26d9)\n\n* fix(web): reset project folders during render, not in an effect (#3649)\n\nmrcfps flagged that the effect-based folder reset still let the new\nDesignFilesPanel (keyed by projectId) mount once with the previous project's\nfolders before the effect cleared them — briefly suppressing the new project's\nempty state, the exact regression this follow-up removes. When the fork point is an assistant turn whose run\nerrored or had its connection reset before the message was written to the\ndatabase (common with AMR/ACP &quot;Connection reset by server&quot;), that id is not in\nthe DB. Switching conversations resets that\narray to [] while the new conversation's history is fetched, so the active\nrow rendered a phantom &quot;0 msg&quot; for the duration of the load (and stayed at 0\nif the load failed) instead of its real count.\n\nTrust `messages.length` for the active conversation only once the live array\nactually reflects it (`messagesConversationId === activeConversationId`);\notherwise fall back to the persisted server `messageCount`. Two causes:\n\n1. `.chat-log` is a flex column with no vertical anchoring, so when the content is\n   shorter than the viewport the messages sit at the top (~530px of dead space below).\n2. The anchor-to-top reserve (`.chat-log-tail-spacer`, sized while a reply streams so\n   the user's turn can reach the top) never collapsed once the turn finished. A short\n   completed turn was left pinned at the top above ~540px of reserved blank.\n\nFix:\n- Bottom-anchor the transcript with `margin-top: auto` on the first row. When content\n  is shorter than the viewport the auto margin absorbs the slack and the messages rest\n  against the composer; when it outgrows the viewport the margin resolves to 0 and the\n  log scrolls normally with the top fully reachable. (Deliberately not\n  `justify-content: flex-end`, which clips overflowing content above the scroll range.)\n- Collapse the tail spacer when streaming ends, and reset it (plus the anchor flags) on\n  conversation switch, so a finished or freshly opened short conversation settles cleanly\n  instead of inheriting a stale reserve. Anchor-to-top during an active turn is unchanged.\n\nVerified live (Playwright, local daemon+web): fresh short conversation last-message-to-\nbottom gap 532px -&gt; 20px; completed short turn tail spacer 549px -&gt; 0; long conversation\nstill scrolls and reaches the top (scrollTop 0, first row at +18px).\n\n* fix(web): flip deck preview to a queued send's marked slide (#3668) (#3681)\n\n* fix(web): flip deck preview to a queued send's marked slide\n\nWhen several edits are queued against marked elements on different deck\nslides, the preview stayed parked on slide 1 while the agent drained the\nqueue — the user couldn't see each edit land in context.\n\nThe mark's slide index was already captured into the comment snapshot and\ntarget, but it was dropped when the snapshot became a ChatCommentAttachment,\nso nothing downstream knew which slide a queued send touched. Carry\n`slideIndex` through the contract and both attachment builders, then have\nProjectView arm a `slideNavRequest` (mirroring `shareRequest`) the moment a\nqueued send starts processing. FileWorkspace activates the deck tab if it is\nopen and FileViewer consumes the nonce once to flip the preview via the\nexisting `od:slide` go bridge, writing the cached slide state first so a\nfreshly-mounted iframe restores to the same slide on load.\n\nNo-op for plain prompts, free pins, or marks without a slide index.\n\n* fix(web): keep deck slide-nav consume-once across viewer remounts\n\nThe consumed-nonce guard lived in a per-mount ref, so leaving the deck tab\nand returning remounted HtmlViewer, reset the ref, and replayed the still-live\nslideNavRequest — yanking the preview back to the queued slide and clobbering\nmanual navigation. Move the guard into shouldConsumeSlideNav, keyed by preview\nstate key outside the component, so &quot;consume once&quot; survives remounts. Add a\nunit spec covering first-handling, fresh-nonce, and per-file isolation.\n\n* fix(web): only flip a deck whose tab was open when the queued send started\n\nslideNavRequest stayed live in parent state, so a request for a deck that was\nclosed at processing-start would resurface and jump the preview when the user\nopened that file by hand much later — not the follow-along behavior the feature\npromises. Decide deliverability once, at fire time: FileWorkspace marks the\nnonce deliverable only when the target deck is already an open tab, and the\nviewer is fed the request only for that deliverable nonce on the active file.\nA closed deck never becomes deliverable, so opening it later cannot jump.\n\nPure helpers isSlideNavDeliverableNow / deliverableSlideNavForActiveFile carry\nthe decision, with specs for the closed-deck-opened-later case.\n\n* Fix URL preview image export capture\n\n* fix(web): keep generation progress card on the design-files tab (#3671) (#3680)\n\n* fix(web): keep generation progress card on the design-files tab (#3671)\n\nThe right-side preview pane showed the empty &quot;Creations will appear\nhere&quot; file list while an agent was actively generating, instead of the\ngeneration progress card with its understand/generate/prepare steps.\n\nPR #3516 (Lexical composer rewrite) added an `activeTab !==\nDESIGN_FILES_TAB` clause to `showGenerationPreview`. The design-files\ntab is the default landing tab, so on a fresh project the in-flight\ngeneration preview was suppressed and the idle empty state won the\nsurface. Before #3516 the preview branch rendered ahead of the\ndesign-files branch with no tab guard.\n\nDrop the clause so the progress card again takes priority over the empty\nfile list. `buildGenerationPreviewState` returns null once a previewable\nartifact exists, so a finished artifact is never hidden behind the card.\n\nAdds a red-spec FileWorkspace test that renders a generating run on the\ndesign-files tab and asserts the generation-preview stage is shown.\n\n* fix(web): scope generation-preview override to the empty design-files tab\n\nAddress review: dropping the DESIGN_FILES_TAB guard outright let the\ngeneration card win for every in-flight run on the design-files tab,\nhiding the file browser of a populated project until a previewable\nartifact existed. Scope the override to the truly empty tab, mirroring\nDesignFilesPanel's own empty-state gate (no files, no live artifacts, no\nfolders), so a populated project keeps browsing while generating.\n\nAdds a regression test that a populated design-files tab still renders\nfile rows while a run is active.\n\n* feat(web): simplify Home composer (drop plugin-inputs form &amp; example pill, move Design Agent next to Send) (#3645) (#3683)\n\n* feat(web): simplify Home composer (drop plugin-inputs form &amp; example pill, move Design Agent next to Send)\n\n- Remove the inline plugin-inputs form (ARTIFACT KIND / AUDIENCE / TEMPLATE) from the Home composer; required inputs no longer gate Send (defaults still flow to the backend).\n- Remove the dismissible &quot;selected example&quot; pill above the editor; picking an example still seeds the prompt.\n- Move the Design Agent toggle from the footer-left group to sit directly left of the Send button.\n\n* fix(web): keep client-side required-input gate on Home composer\n\nDropping the inline plugin-inputs form left pluginInputsAreValid() always\nreturning true, so a required field with no default that the user blanks out in\nan editable Home scenario kept Send enabled and only failed at click-time apply\nwith a generic &quot;Failed to apply …&quot; error (the daemon's validateInputs rejects\nmissing required inputs).\n\nRestore a client-side gate that mirrors the daemon's required-input rule and\nnames the missing field in the submit error, so these Home flows fail fast.\nExtracts the pure check into utils/pluginRequiredInputs with unit coverage.\n\n* fix(web): drop example from active-row mount condition\n\nWith the example pill removed from the Home composer active row, keeping\nselectedPromptExample in showActiveContextRow mounted an empty .home-hero__active\nrow when an example was picked with no other active context. Remove it from the\ncondition so the row only renders when it has visible content.\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: elihahah666 &lt;template@refly.ai&gt;\nCo-authored-by: qiongyu1999 &lt;2694684348@qq.com&gt;\n\n* ci(release): throttle the per-push nightly build instead of debouncing it (#3682) (#3687)\n\n`notify-release-feishu` builds a full nightly (via release-stable, channel=nightly)\nand posts a Feishu card on every push to `release/**`. Its concurrency used\n`cancel-in-progress: true`, so a newer push cancelled the in-flight build. When a\nrelease branch is busy — pushes spaced closer than a full cross-platform build\ntakes — every build was cancelled by the next push and no nightly was ever cut.\n(Observed on release/v0.10.0: a run of pushes all cancelled, the last successful\nnightly was hours old.)\n\nSwitch to `cancel-in-progress: false` (throttle): the running build finishes, and\npushes that arrive during it coalesce — GitHub keeps the in-progress run plus only\nthe newest pending run, cancelling older pending ones. A busy release branch now\nalways cuts a nightly and converges on the latest commit, with at most one build\nrunning plus one queued. Trade-off: a newer push no longer interrupts a running\nbuild for an older commit; it waits one build cycle. For nightly that's the right\ncall.\n\n* feat(desktop): play white brand splash video on startup (#3674) (#3689)\n\n* feat(desktop): play white brand splash video on startup\n\nReplace the dark &quot;Waiting for the web runtime URL…&quot; placeholder with the\nbrand intro animation on a white background. The clip plays once and then\nfreezes on the settled &quot;Open design&quot; logo. The window only swaps to the web\nruntime after the clip has had time to play through (MIN_SPLASH_MS), so it\nnever flips mid-animation, and it holds the final frame when the runtime\ntakes longer to boot.\n\nThe clip is embedded as a base64 data URL so it renders identically in dev\nand in packaged builds without any electron-builder asset wiring — the\npackaged app exposes no reliable on-disk asset path from the desktop main\nbundle (the previous icon lookup already silently fell back to null there).\n\n* feat(desktop): show splash in parallel with boot, reveal on web mount\n\nMake the white brand splash a dedicated window so it can do real work:\n\n- Packaged cold start now creates the splash BEFORE awaiting the daemon/web\n  sidecars, so the animation is on screen during the whole boot instead of the\n  user staring at no window at all for a few seconds. The 2s minimum splash now\n  overlaps startup rather than being added on top of it.\n- The main window starts hidden and loads the web app behind the splash. We\n  reveal it only once the web bundle reports it has actually mounted\n  (data-od-app-mounted=&quot;1&quot;) and the minimum splash time has elapsed, then close\n  the splash. This removes the dark &quot;Loading Open Design…&quot; web shell that used\n  to flash between the splash and the app. A hard timeout guarantees the user is\n  never stranded on the splash if the mount signal never arrives.\n\ntools-dev/dev keeps working unchanged: when no splash window is handed in, the\nruntime creates its own.\n\n* fix(desktop): measure splash hold from window creation, not adoption\n\nPerishCode review: in packaged startup the splash window is created before\n`await startPackagedSidecars(...)`, but the minimum-hold clock was stamped later\ninside `createDesktopRuntime` (after the sidecars had finished booting). The\nreveal then waited `MIN_SPLASH_MS` from that post-boot instant, so a fast web\nmount still added almost another 2s on top of boot — re-introducing the very\ndelay the parallel splash was meant to remove.\n\n`createSplashWindow()` now returns a `{ window, startedAt }` handle stamped at\ncreation; the packaged entry forwards `startedAt` via `splashStartedAt` and the\nruntime measures the hold from there. Runtime-created dev splashes stamp their\nown creation time, so the hold overlaps boot in every path.\n\n* fix(desktop): gate DesktopRuntime.show() behind the splash reveal\n\nPerishCode review: the main window now starts hidden and is revealed only after\nthe web app mounts, but `DesktopRuntime.show()` still called `window.show()`\nunconditionally. The packaged entry routes a pending second-instance focus\nthrough that method via `onDesktopReady`, so launching the app twice while the\nsidecars/web are still booting would surface the half-loaded main window and\nbypass the reveal gate — reintroducing the startup flash.\n\n`show()` now focuses the splash while the runtime is pre-reveal and only shows\nthe main window once `revealWhenReady()` has fired.\n\n* fix deck keyboard pagination double step\n\n* Fix official plugin and skill catalog search\n\n* fix(web): clear plugin context after send\n\n* fix(web): gate bundled plugin provenance in catalog\n\nGenerated-By: looper 0.9.3 (runner=fixer, agent=codex)\n\n---------\n\nCo-authored-by: yinjialu &lt;2481044901@qq.com&gt;\nCo-authored-by: Ray Xi daemon stays on :7457) is\n403'd by the daemon's same-origin gate until told about that origin.\n\nAdd a callout under section (3)'s table, expand section (4) with a\ndecision table covering class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Open Design: The open-source Claude Design alternative</h1><a id=\"user-content-open-design-the-open-source-claude-design-alternative\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Open Design: The open-source Claude Design alternative\" href=\"#open-design-the-open-source-claude-design-alternative\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<blockquote>\n<p dir=\"auto\">🔥 <strong>Open Design 0.10.0 is here: the all-in-one Agentic design workspace.</strong> The whole craft now lives in one window — go from a vague idea to discovering references, gathering material, editing interactively, queuing comments, polishing motion, and handing off to an editor or a Code Agent — without leaving the app. With parallel sessions, it stops feeling like one assistant and starts feeling like a local design team working for you. <a href=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases\">Download 0.10.0</a> · <a href=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/discussions/4153\" data-hovercard-type=\"discussion\" data-hovercard-url=\"/nexu-io/open-design/discussions/4153/hovercard\" aria-keyshortcuts=\"Alt+ArrowUp\">Join the discussion</a></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\">⚡ <strong>Open Design AMR (Agentic Model Router) — the official model service.</strong> One recharge to use GPT, Claude, Gemini, and DeepSeek inside Open Design: 20+ flagship models, zero config, billed by real token usage. <a href=\"https://open-design.ai/amr/\" rel=\"nofollow\">Try AMR</a></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\">🏅 <strong>The Open Design Fellow program is now open.</strong> If you also believe design should be open — become an Open Design Fellow, shape the product alongside the core team, and help more people take part in defining the future of design. Details → <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/MAINTAINERS.md\"><code>MAINTAINERS.md</code></a> and <a href=\"https://discord.gg/qhbcCH8Am4\" rel=\"nofollow\">Discord</a>.</p>\n</blockquote>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><h2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">What is Open Design</h2><a id=\"user-content-what-is-open-design\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What is Open Design\" href=\"#what-is-open-design\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\">🎨 <strong>The local-first, open-source <a href=\"https://x.com/claudeai/status/2045156267690213649\" rel=\"nofollow\">Claude Design</a> alternative.</strong> &nbsp;🖥️ <strong>Native desktop app for macOS and Windows.</strong> &nbsp;⚡ <strong>100+ skills</strong> · ✨ <strong>150 brand-grade <code>DESIGN.md</code> systems</strong> · 📦 <strong>261 ready-to-use plugins.</strong> &nbsp;🖼️ Generates <strong>web · desktop · mobile prototypes</strong>, <strong>live dashboards / artifacts</strong>, <strong>decks</strong>, <strong>images</strong>, <strong>video</strong>, plus <strong>HyperFrames</strong> motion graphics. 🔒 Sandboxed iframe preview · HTML / PDF / PPTX / MP4 export. &nbsp;🤖 <strong>Runs on Claude Code · OpenClaw · Codex · Cursor · OpenCode · Qwen · Copilot · Amp · Hermes · Kimi · Antigravity and 22 local CLIs</strong>, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via BYOK.</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Open Design is what you get when the <strong>agent-native</strong> loop Anthropic shipped with Claude Design — discover the brief, lock the direction, stream the artifact, critique, deliver — stops being closed and becomes a <strong>filesystem of skills, design systems, and plugins</strong> that the coding agents already on your laptop can read, write, and remix. Your CLI becomes the design engine, your laptop becomes the studio, and your team's <code>DESIGN.md</code> becomes the brand contract.</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\">It's also the <strong>Figma alternative for the agent era</strong> — instead of pushing pixels on a canvas, it delivers single-page artifacts in real CSS, real fonts, real components, exported straight to HTML / PDF / PPTX / MP4 — already shaped by your design system, already runnable inside the agent you use every day.</p>\n<hr>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Product tour</h2><a id=\"user-content-product-tour\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Product tour\" href=\"#product-tour\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\">A quick look at what Open Design is and what it does. Start from <strong>Home</strong>, orchestrate repeat workflows with <strong>Automation</strong>, distill a brand contract in <strong>Design System</strong>, and extend with <strong>Plugins</strong> and <strong>integrations</strong>; inside any project's <strong>Studio</strong>, the same design system streams out prototypes, live artifacts, HyperFrames, decks, and images.</p>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Core pages</h3><a id=\"user-content-core-pages\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Core pages\" href=\"#core-pages\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<markdown-accessiblity-table><table>\n<tbody><tr>\n<td valign=\"top\">\n<a target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer nofollow\" href=\"https://camo.githubusercontent.com/df9488e882b012f422ce712abe5c418ef191b99f508e4aef64f2ab0b260e0fb0/68747470733a2f2f7265706f2d6173736574732e6f70656e2d64657369676e2e61692f7265736f75726365732f696d616765732f70726f647563742f686f6d652e706e67\"><img src=\"https://camo.githubusercontent.com/df9488e882b012f422ce712abe5c418ef191b99f508e4aef64f2ab0b260e0fb0/68747470733a2f2f7265706f2d6173736574732e6f70656e2d64657369676e2e61692f7265736f75726365732f696d616765732f70726f647563742f686f6d652e706e67\" alt=\"Home page\" data-canonical-src=\"https://repo-assets.open-design.ai/resources/images/product/home.png\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"></a><br>\n<sub><b>Home</b> — the overview entry point. Pick a skill and a design system, type the brief, and kick off everything from one place.</sub>\n</td>\n</tr>\n</tbody></table></markdown-accessiblity-table>\n<markdown-accessiblity-table><table>\n<tbody><tr>\n<td width=\"50%\" valign=\"top\">\n<a target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Platform Compatibility</h2><a id=\"user-content-platform-compatibility\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Platform Compatibility\" href=\"#platform-compatibility\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 ships as <strong>skills, a CLI, and an MCP server</strong> that mainstream coding agents consume natively. Once OD is installed, a single <code>od mcp install &lt;agent&gt;</code> wires the MCP server into that agent's config, and you call the same tools from inside any agent.</p>\n</blockquote>\n<markdown-accessiblity-table><table>\n<thead>\n<tr>\n<th>Coding agent / platform &nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</th>\n<th align=\"center\">Status remove · full list with <code>od mcp install --help</code>.</p>\n<p align=\"center\" dir=\"auto\">\n  <a target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer nofollow\" href=\"https://camo.githubusercontent.com/67e5284a287c972b89b8e205f8f3a1c7ea5b104bc9ff178f5549e4010de5366d/68747470733a2f2f7265706f2d6173736574732e6f70656e2d64657369676e2e61692f7265736f75726365732f696d616765732f636f64696e672d6167656e74732e706e67\"><img src=\"https://camo.githubusercontent.com/67e5284a287c972b89b8e205f8f3a1c7ea5b104bc9ff178f5549e4010de5366d/68747470733a2f2f7265706f2d6173736574732e6f70656e2d64657369676e2e61692f7265736f75726365732f696d616765732f636f64696e672d6167656e74732e706e67\" alt=\"The 21 coding-agent CLIs Open Design supports — Claude Code · Codex · OpenCode · Hermes · Antigravity · Gemini · Grok Build · Kimi · Cursor Agent · Qwen · Qoder · GitHub Copilot · Pi · Kiro · Kilo · Mistral Vibe · DeepSeek · Reasonix · Aider · Devin · Trae\" width=\"100%\" data-canonical-src=\"https://repo-assets.open-design.ai/resources/images/coding-agents.png\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"></a>\n</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>No CLI installed?</strong> The BYOK proxy at <code>POST /api/proxy/{anthropic,openai,azure,google,ollama,senseaudio}/stream</code> gives you the same loop (no process spawn) — paste <code>baseUrl</code> + <code>apiKey</code> + <code>model</code>, with support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Per-target SSRF protection blocks internal IPs / link-local / CGNAT at the daemon edge.</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\">The adapter contract and stream parsers live in <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/apps/daemon/src/agents.ts\"><code>apps/daemon/src/agents.ts</code></a>. Adding a new CLI is one entry — see <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/agent-adapters.md\"><code>docs/agent-adapters.md</code></a>.</p>\n<hr>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Demo</h2><a id=\"user-content-demo\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Demo\" href=\"#demo\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Four core product categories, all rendered by a coding agent running on your laptop. Click a thumbnail to see the real example.</p>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">1 · Prototypes — web · desktop · mobile</h3><a id=\"user-content-1--prototypes--web--desktop--mobile\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 1 · Prototypes — web · desktop · mobile\" href=\"#1--prototypes--web--desktop--mobile\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\">The default output surface. Single-page HTML artifacts that read your <code>DESIGN.md</code> and render in a sandboxed iframe.</p>\n<markdown-accessiblity-table><table>\n<tbody><tr>\n<td width=\"50%\" valign=\"top\">\n<a target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/screenshots/01-entry-view.png\"><img src=\"/nexu-io/open-design/raw/main/docs/screenshots/01-entry-view.png\" alt=\"Entry view\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"></a><br>\n<sub><b>Entry view</b> — pick a skill, pick a design system, type the brief. The agent never redraws the phone frame; shared device frames live in <code>assets/frames/</code>.</sub>\n</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td width=\"50%\" valign=\"top\">\n<a target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/screenshots/skills/dating-web.png\"><img src=\"/nexu-io/open-design/raw/main/docs/screenshots/skills/dating-web.png\" alt=\"Web prototype dating-web\" style=\"max-width: 100%;\"></a><br>\n<sub><b>Web prototype</b> — an editorial dashboard with scrollbars, KPIs, and charts. The agent writes HTML + CSS + GSAP, and HyperFrames renders it to a deterministic MP4 via headless Chrome + FFmpeg. Pair it with <strong>Seedance 2.0</strong> for cinematic t2v / i2v, <strong>Veo 3 / Sora 2 / Kling 2</strong> for routed model variants, and <strong>Suno v5 / Lyria 2</strong> for the audio src=\"https://camo.githubusercontent.com/0d12d94c829897cc96d253ceb41dfdfdb4dac4fcaa2215afd8f0abeb4bd9a112/68747470733a2f2f7374617469632e68657967656e2e61692f68797065726672616d65732d6f73732f646f63732f696d616765732f636174616c6f672f626c6f636b732f696e7374616772616d2d666f6c6c6f772e706e67\" · 16:9 · captures the site at 3 viewports</sub></td>\n</tr>\n</tbody></table></markdown-accessiblity-table>\n<p dir=\"auto\">11 HyperFrames templates + 39 Seedance prompts ship with the repo. Catalog thumbnails © HeyGen; the framework is Apache-2.0. The OD-specific render workflow (composition cache, sandbox-exec workaround, MP4-as-chip) is detailed in <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/design-templates/hyperframes\"><code>design-templates/hyperframes/</code></a>.</p>\n<hr>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Why Open Design</h2><a id=\"user-content-why-open-design\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Why Open Design\" href=\"#why-open-design\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<blockquote>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>In April 2026, Anthropic released <a href=\"https://x.com/claudeai/status/2045156267690213649\" rel=\"nofollow\">Claude Design</a> — the first time an LLM stopped writing prose and started delivering design artifacts directly.</strong> It went viral. But it stayed closed-source, paid-only, cloud-only, locked to Anthropic's model, Anthropic's skills, Anthropic's surface. No checkout, no self-host, no Vercel deploy, no swap-in-your-own-agent.</p>\n</blockquote>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Open Design (OD) is the open-source alternative. Same loop, same artifact-first mental model, none of the lock-in:</p>\n<ul dir=\"auto\">\n<li>🤖 <strong>Agent-native, model-agnostic.</strong> We don't ship an agent. The <code>claude</code> / <code>codex</code> / <code>cursor-agent</code> / <code>copilot</code> / <code>hermes</code> / <code>kimi</code> already on your <code>PATH</code> are the design engine. Swap with one click.</li>\n<li>🧠 <strong>Brand-grade by default.</strong> Every render reads the active <code>DESIGN.md</code> — a 9-section schema covering palette, type, spacing, motion, voice, anti-patterns. 150 systems ship with the repo (Linear, Stripe, Vercel, Airbnb, Apple, Tesla, Notion, Anthropic, Cursor, Supabase, Figma…). Drop a folder in, the picker finds it.</li>\n<li>🖥️ <strong>Local-first, BYOK at every layer.</strong> Native desktop apps for macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel) and Windows (x64). Linux AppImage on the optional release lane. SQLite at <code>.od/app.sqlite</code>, files at <code>.od/projects/&lt;id&gt;/</code>, no telemetry, no cloud round-trip.</li>\n<li>🌍 <strong>Composable on three planes.</strong> <strong>Plugins</strong> carry runnable workflows · <strong>skills</strong> carry the agent's design taste · <strong>design systems</strong> carry the brand. All three are plain files anyone can author, version, and publish.</li>\n<li>🔁 <strong>Refresh an existing codebase.</strong> Hand a <code>git</code> repo + <code>DESIGN.md</code> to the agent and it refactors your real components to the brand spec. Dedicated plugins migrate Figma / Pencil workflows into React / Next.js / Vue code.</li>\n<li>🔒 <strong>Privacy by conviction.</strong> Everything runs where your data lives — your laptop, your team's server, your Vercel project. When the network is needed, the BYOK proxy is SSRF-guarded.</li>\n</ul>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Comparison</h3><a id=\"user-content-comparison\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Comparison\" href=\"#comparison\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<markdown-accessiblity-table><table>\n<thead>\n<tr>\n<th></th>\n<th><a href=\"https://x.com/claudeai/status/2045156267690213649\" rel=\"nofollow\">Claude shipped</strong></td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td>Skills / plugins / templates</td>\n<td>Closed</td>\n<td>Plugin store</td>\n<td>Closed</td>\n<td><strong>✅ 100+ skills · 261 No Node, no pnpm, no clone.</p>\n<ul dir=\"auto\">\n<li><strong>macOS</strong> (Apple Silicon · Intel x64) → <a href=\"https://open-design.ai/\" rel=\"nofollow\"><strong>open-design.ai</strong></a> or <a href=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases\">GitHub Releases</a></li>\n<li><strong>Windows</strong> (x64) → <a href=\"https://open-design.ai/\" href=\"https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design/releases\">GitHub Releases</a></li>\n</ul>\n<p dir=\"auto\">After install: the app auto-detects every coding-agent CLI on your <code>PATH</code>, loads 100+ skills and 150 design systems, and lets you type a brief in the entry view.</p>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">🤖 Install binds the <code>DESIGN.md</code> you named, and emits an <code>&lt;artifact&gt;</code> previewable at <code>http://localhost:7456</code>.</p>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z\"></path>\n</svg>\n    </clipboard-copy>\n  </div></div>\n<blockquote>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>macOS users:</strong> If the web UI shows <code>Authorization: Bearer &lt;OD_API_TOKEN&gt; required</code>, Docker Desktop bridge networking is the cause. See <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/deploy/README.md#docker-desktop-on-macos\">Docker Desktop on macOS</a> for the fix.</p>\n</blockquote>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">🧑‍💻 Run from source</h3><a id=\"user-content-‍-run-from-source\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🧑‍💻 Run from source\" href=\"#‍-run-from-source\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<div class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\"><pre>git clone https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design.git\n<span class=\"pl-c1\">cd</span> open-design\ncorepack <span class=\"pl-c1\">enable</span> <span class=\"pl-k\">&amp;&amp;</span> pnpm install\npnpm tools-dev run web</pre><div class=\"zeroclipboard-container\">\n    <clipboard-copy aria-label=\"Copy code to clipboard\" class=\"ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center\" data-copy-feedback=\"Copied!\" data-tooltip-direction=\"w\" value=\"git clone https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design.git\ncd open-design\ncorepack enable &amp;&amp; Windows users, see <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/windows-troubleshooting.md\"><code>docs/windows-troubleshooting.md</code></a>. Full quickstart, env vars, Nix flake, and packaged build flow → <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/QUICKSTART.md\"><code>QUICKSTART.md</code></a>.</p>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">A full workflow — from brief to artifact</h3><a id=\"user-content-a-full-workflow--from-brief-to-artifact\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: A full workflow — from brief to artifact\" href=\"#a-full-workflow--from-brief-to-artifact\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><code>brief → plugin → direction → design system → artifact → handoff → memory</code></p>\n<ol dir=\"auto\">\n<li><strong>A PM submits a brief.</strong> The plugin picker offers landing page · pitch deck · dashboard · social post · PM spec · OKR scorecard…</li>\n<li><strong>A designer (or the agent) locks the direction.</strong> No brand? Pick from 5 curated directions. Have a brand? Drop a screenshot / URL → the agent connects GitHub, imports Figma, and codifies a reusable <code>DESIGN.md</code>.</li>\n<li><strong>The agent emits the first <code>&lt;artifact&gt;</code>.</strong> Plugin + skill + <code>DESIGN.md</code> are bound. It streams into a sandboxed iframe, editable in place — not \"regenerate from scratch.\"</li>\n<li><strong>Hand off to engineering.</strong> The artifact is real HTML/CSS — drop it into Cursor, Codex, or Claude Code to keep building as code. Or export PPTX / PDF / MP4 straight to marketing.</li>\n<li><strong>Open Design gets smarter as you use it.</strong> Your screenshots, fonts, palettes, and confirmed artifacts accumulate as defaults for the next session. Less rework, less drift.</li>\n</ol>\n<hr>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Use Open Design from your coding agent</h2><a id=\"user-content-use-open-design-from-your-coding-agent\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Use Open Design from your coding agent\" href=\"#use-open-design-from-your-coding-agent\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Open Design ships a <strong>stdio MCP server</strong> and per-agent <strong>install scripts</strong>. Any MCP-compatible agent in another repo can read files from your local Open Design projects directly — tokens CSS, JSX components, entry HTML — as a structured API queryable by name. The agent always sees the live file, not a stale export.</p>\n<div class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\"><pre><span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> One-line install (16+ CLIs supported):</span>\ncurl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh <span class=\"pl-k\">|</span> sh -s <span class=\"pl-k\">&lt;</span>agent<span class=\"pl-k\">&gt;</span>\n\n<span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> Then the agent can:</span>\nod search-files <span class=\"pl-s\"><span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span>primary button<span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span></span>      <span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> search files across projects</span>\nod get-file design-systems/linear-app/DESIGN.md\nod get-artifact <span class=\"pl-k\">&lt;</span>slug<span class=\"pl-k\">&gt;</span>                <span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> latest rendered artifact</span>\nod plugin run web-prototype --brief <span class=\"pl-s\"><span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span>...<span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span></span>\nod skill list --scenario marketing</pre><div class=\"zeroclipboard-container\">\n    <clipboard-copy aria-label=\"Copy code to clipboard\" class=\"ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center\" data-copy-feedback=\"Copied!\" data-tooltip-direction=\"w\" value=\"# One-line install (16+ CLIs supported):\ncurl -fsSL https://open-design.ai/install.sh | sh -s &lt;agent&gt;\n\n# Then the agent can:\nod search-files &quot;primary button&quot;      # search files across projects\nod get-file design-systems/linear-app/DESIGN.md\nod get-artifact &lt;slug&gt;                # latest rendered artifact\nod plugin run web-prototype --brief &quot;...&quot;\nod skill list --scenario marketing\" tabindex=\"0\" role=\"button\">\n      <svg aria-hidden=\"true\" data-component=\"Octicon\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" data-view-component=\"true\" class=\"octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon\">\n    <path d=\"M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z\"></path><path d=\"M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z\"></path>\n</svg>\n      <svg aria-hidden=\"true\" data-component=\"Octicon\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" data-view-component=\"true\" class=\"octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none\">\n    <path d=\"M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z\"></path>\n</svg>\n    </clipboard-copy>\n  </div></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>Why MCP?</strong> Exporting and re-attaching a zip every iteration breaks flow. MCP exposes the design source directly — the agent always sees the live file.</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>For an agent starting from scratch,</strong> the installer places <code>~/.config/&lt;agent&gt;/open-design.json</code> (or the platform equivalent) plus a copy-paste MCP snippet. Cursor gets a one-click deeplink; Claude Code gets a <code>claude mcp add-json</code> one-liner; every other agent gets JSON in the schema its config expects. Full per-agent flow → <strong>Settings → MCP server</strong> in the desktop app, or <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/agent-adapters.md\"><code>docs/agent-adapters.md</code></a>.</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>Security model.</strong> Read-only by default, the daemon binds to <code>127.0.0.1</code>, and SSRF is blocked at the proxy edge. LAN exposure requires an explicit <code>OD_BIND_HOST</code> plus <code>OD_ALLOWED_ORIGINS</code>. Connector credentials and live-artifact preview routes stay loopback-only regardless.</p>\n<hr>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Skills</h2><a id=\"user-content-skills\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Skills\" href=\"#skills\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>100+ skills ship in the box</strong> — each is a folder under <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/skills\"><code>skills/</code></a> following the Claude Code <a href=\"https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code/skills\" rel=\"nofollow\"><code>SKILL.md</code></a> convention, extended with an <code>od:</code> frontmatter (<code>mode</code>, <code>platform</code>, <code>scenario</code>, <code>preview.type</code>, <code>design_system.requires</code>, <code>default_for</code>, <code>fidelity</code>, <code>example_prompt</code>). Drop a folder in, restart the daemon, it appears in the picker.</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Two <strong>modes</strong> anchor the catalog: <code>prototype</code> (web/mobile/desktop single-page artifacts) and <code>deck</code> (horizontal-swipe presentations). Also <code>image</code>, <code>video</code>, <code>audio</code>, <code>template</code>, <code>design-system</code>, and <code>utility</code> modes. The <strong><code>scenario</code></strong> field groups them by audience: <code>design</code> · <code>marketing</code> · <code>operation</code> · <code>engineering</code> · <code>product</code> · <code>finance</code> · <code>hr</code> · <code>sale</code> · <code>personal</code>.</p>\n<markdown-accessiblity-table><table>\n<thead>\n<tr>\n<th>Skill</th>\n<th>Mode</th>\n<th>Scenario</th>\n<th>What Skill registry endpoint: <code>GET /api/skills</code>.</p>\n<hr>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Design Systems</h2><a id=\"user-content-design-systems\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Design Systems\" href=\"#design-systems\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>150 brand-grade <code>DESIGN.md</code> systems</strong> ship with the repo — each a single Markdown file with a 9-section schema (color, typography, spacing, layout, components, motion, voice, brand, anti-patterns), from <a href=\"https://github.com/VoltAgent/awesome-design-md\"><code>VoltAgent/awesome-design-md</code></a>. Switch a system → the next render uses the new tokens. No theme JSON.</p>\n<details>\n<summary><b>Full catalog (click to expand)</b></summary>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>AI &amp; LLM</strong> — <code>claude</code> · <code>cohere</code> · <code>mistral-ai</code> · <code>minimax</code> · <code>together-ai</code> · <code>replicate</code> · <code>runwayml</code> · <code>elevenlabs</code> · <code>ollama</code> · <code>x-ai</code></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>Developer Tools</strong> — <code>cursor</code> · <code>vercel</code> · <code>linear-app</code> · <code>framer</code> · <code>expo</code> · <code>clickhouse</code> · <code>mongodb</code> · <code>supabase</code> · <code>hashicorp</code> · <code>posthog</code> · <code>sentry</code> · <code>warp</code> · <code>webflow</code> · <code>sanity</code> · <code>mintlify</code> · <code>lovable</code> · <code>composio</code> · <code>opencode-ai</code> · <code>voltagent</code></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>Productivity</strong> — <code>notion</code> · <code>figma</code> · <code>miro</code> · <code>airtable</code> · <code>superhuman</code> · <code>intercom</code> · <code>zapier</code> · <code>cal</code> · <code>clay</code> · <code>raycast</code></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>Fintech</strong> — <code>stripe</code> · <code>coinbase</code> · <code>binance</code> · <code>kraken</code> · <code>mastercard</code> · <code>revolut</code> · <code>wise</code></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>E-commerce</strong> — <code>shopify</code> · <code>airbnb</code> · <code>uber</code> · <code>nike</code> · <code>starbucks</code> · <code>pinterest</code></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>Media</strong> — <code>spotify</code> · <code>playstation</code> · <code>wired</code> · <code>theverge</code> · <code>meta</code></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>Automotive</strong> — <code>tesla</code> · <code>bmw</code> · <code>ferrari</code> · <code>lamborghini</code> · <code>bugatti</code> · <code>renault</code></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>Other</strong> — <code>apple</code> · <code>ibm</code> · <code>nvidia</code> · <code>vodafone</code> · <code>resend</code> · <code>spacex</code></p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>Starters</strong> — <code>default</code> (Neutral Modern) · <code>warm-editorial</code></p>\n</details>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Re-import the library via <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/scripts/sync-design-systems.ts\"><code>scripts/sync-design-systems.ts</code></a>. Add your own brand → drop a <code>DESIGN.md</code> into <code>design-systems/&lt;brand&gt;/</code>. Full guide → <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/design-systems/README.md\"><code>design-systems/README.md</code></a>.</p>\n<hr>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Plugins</h2><a id=\"user-content-plugins\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Plugins\" href=\"#plugins\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>261 official plugins</strong> live in <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/_official\"><code>plugins/_official/</code></a>. Each plugin is a <strong>portable agent-skill folder</strong> — a <code>SKILL.md</code> (readable by any agent that supports Agent Skills), plus an optional <code>open-design.json</code> manifest that gives Open Design marketplace metadata, inputs, previews, pipelines, and capability declarations. Jump straight to a category:</p>\n<markdown-accessiblity-table><table>\n<thead>\n<tr>\n<th>Category</th>\n<th>Count</th>\n<th>Contents</th>\n</tr>\n</thead>\n<tbody>\n<tr>\n<td><a community plugins and <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/registry\"><code>plugins/registry/</code></a> for the publishing flow.</p>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">What plugins can do</h3><a id=\"user-content-what-plugins-can-do\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: What plugins can do\" href=\"#what-plugins-can-do\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<ul dir=\"auto\">\n<li>🤖 <strong>Run in any coding agent</strong> — <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/docs/agent-adapters.md\">Claude Code</a>, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, <a href=\"https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw\">OpenClaw</a>, <a href=\"https://antigravity.google\" rel=\"nofollow\">Antigravity</a>, Hermes, Kimi… through the same skill protocol the agent already knows.</li>\n<li>🔁 <strong>Migrate Figma / Pencil workflows</strong> → React, Next.js, or Vue source. See <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/_official/scenarios/od-figma-migration\"><code>od-figma-migration</code></a>.</li>\n<li>🛠️ <strong>Refresh an existing codebase to a brand spec</strong> — point a plugin at a <code>git</code> repo + <code>DESIGN.md</code>, get a PR. See <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/_official/scenarios/od-code-migration\"><code>od-code-migration</code></a>.</li>\n<li>💾 <strong>Persist custom workflows</strong> — your team's reusable templates sit next to the shipped ones.</li>\n</ul>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Using plugins</h3><a id=\"user-content-using-plugins\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Using plugins\" href=\"#using-plugins\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Plugins are at full parity across the <strong>web UI</strong> and the <strong><code>od</code> CLI</strong> — same <code>/api/plugins</code> endpoints, pick whichever fits.</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>In the desktop / web app:</strong> open the <strong>Plugin</strong> page to browse the marketplace and click <strong>Install</strong>; inside a project's Studio, plugins appear as composer chips you click to apply (with the inputs they declare).</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\"><strong>On the command line</strong> (runs without a UI — this is the path external agents use):</p>\n<div class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\"><pre>od plugin list                       <span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> list installed plugins (--task-kind / --mode / --tag filters)</span>\nod plugin search <span class=\"pl-s\"><span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span>landing page<span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span></span>      <span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> search by keyword</span>\nod plugin info od-default            <span class=\"pl-c\"><span accepts ./local-folder or an https://… link</span>\nod plugin apply od-default --input brief=<span class=\"pl-s\"><span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span>a one-page pitch for our seed round<span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span></span>\nod plugin upgrade od-default         <span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> upgrade</span>\nod plugin uninstall od-default       <span <code>--json</code>, so you can pipe it through <code>jq</code> / <code>xargs</code> into automation.</p>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Building a plugin</h3><a id=\"user-content-building-a-plugin\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Building a plugin\" href=\"#building-a-plugin\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\">A plugin <strong>needs only a <code>SKILL.md</code> at minimum</strong>; to list it in the Open Design marketplace, add an <code>open-design.json</code>:</p>\n<div class=\"snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\"><pre class=\"notranslate\"><code>my-plugin/\n├── SKILL.md            ← required: YAML frontmatter (name · description) + trigger phrasing + workflow (aim for &lt; 500 lines)\n├── open-design.json    ← needed to list: marketplace metadata + inputs + pipeline + capabilities\n├── README.md           ← optional: usage, install, registry links\n├── preview/            ← optional: index.html / poster.png (strongly recommended for visual plugins)\n└── examples/           ← optional: concrete use cases\n</code></pre><div class=\"zeroclipboard-container\">\n    <clipboard-copy aria-label=\"Copy code to clipboard\" class=\"ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible README.md           ← optional: usage, install, registry links\n├── preview/            ← optional: index.html / poster.png (strongly recommended for visual plugins)\n└── examples/           ← optional: concrete use cases\" tabindex=\"0\" role=\"button\">\n      <svg aria-hidden=\"true\" data-component=\"Octicon\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\" data-component=\"Octicon\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" data-view-component=\"true\" class=\"octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none\">\n    <path d=\"M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z\"></path>\n</svg>\n    </clipboard-copy>\n  </div></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Core <code>open-design.json</code> fields: <code>specVersion</code> (currently <code>1.0.0</code>), <code>name</code> (stable ID), <code>version</code> (semver), <code>compat.agentSkills[].path</code> (points at <code>./SKILL.md</code>), <code>od.kind</code> (<code>skill</code> / <code>scenario</code> / <code>atom</code> / <code>bundle</code>), <code>od.taskKind</code> (<code>new-generation</code> / <code>figma-migration</code> / <code>code-migration</code> / <code>tune-collab</code>), <code>od.mode</code> (the output surface, e.g. <code>prototype</code> / <code>deck</code> / <code>live-artifact</code> / <code>image</code> / <code>video</code> / <code>hyperframes</code> / <code>audio</code> / <code>design-system</code> / <code>scenario</code>), <code>od.capabilities[]</code> (<strong>declare the minimum</strong> — a restricted install grants only <code>prompt:inject</code> by default), <code>od.inputs[]</code> (apply-time parameters).</p>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Scaffold + validate locally:</p>\n<div class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\"><pre>od plugin scaffold --id my-plugin --title <span class=\"pl-s\"><span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span>My Plugin<span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span></span>   <span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> generate the skeleton</span>\nod plugin validate ./my-plugin                          <span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> check manifest / file layout</span>\npnpm guard <span class=\"pl-k\">&amp;&amp;</span> pnpm --filter @open-design/plugin-runtime typecheck</pre><div class=\"zeroclipboard-container\">\n    <clipboard-copy aria-label=\"Copy code to clipboard\" class=\"ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center\" data-copy-feedback=\"Copied!\" data-tooltip-direction=\"w\" value=\"od plugin scaffold --id my-plugin --title &quot;My Plugin&quot;   # generate the skeleton\nod plugin validate ./my-plugin                          # check manifest / file layout\npnpm guard &amp;&amp; pnpm --filter @open-design/plugin-runtime typecheck\" tabindex=\"0\" role=\"button\">\n      <svg aria-hidden=\"true\" data-component=\"Octicon\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" data-view-component=\"true\" class=\"octicon octicon-copy js-clipboard-copy-icon\">\n    <path d=\"M0 6.75C0 5.784.784 5 1.75 5h1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.5h-1.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-1.5a.75.75 0 0 1 1.5 0v1.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 9.25 16h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 0 14.25Z\"></path><path d=\"M5 1.75C5 .784 5.784 0 6.75 0h7.5C15.216 0 16 .784 16 1.75v7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 14.25 11h-7.5A1.75 1.75 0 0 1 5 9.25Zm1.75-.25a.25.25 0 0 0-.25.25v7.5c0 .138.112.25.25.25h7.5a.25.25 0 0 0 .25-.25v-7.5a.25.25 0 0 0-.25-.25Z\"></path>\n</svg>\n      <svg aria-hidden=\"true\" data-component=\"Octicon\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" data-view-component=\"true\" class=\"octicon octicon-check js-clipboard-check-icon color-fg-success d-none\">\n    <path d=\"M13.78 4.22a.75.75 0 0 1 0 1.06l-7.25 7.25a.75.75 0 0 1-1.06 0L2.22 9.28a.751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018L6 10.94l6.72-6.72a.75.75 0 0 1 1.06 0Z\"></path>\n</svg>\n    </clipboard-copy>\n  </div></div>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Full field set and runtime contract → <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/spec/SPEC.md\"><code>plugins/spec/SPEC.md</code></a>; developing a plugin with a coding agent → <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/spec/AGENT-DEVELOPMENT.md\"><code>plugins/spec/AGENT-DEVELOPMENT.md</code></a>; copy-paste minimal templates → <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/spec/examples\"><code>plugins/spec/examples/</code></a>.</p>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">Contributing a plugin</h3><a id=\"user-content-contributing-a-plugin\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: Contributing a plugin\" href=\"#contributing-a-plugin\"><svg data-component=\"Octicon\" class=\"octicon octicon-link\" viewBox=\"0 0 16 16\" version=\"1.1\" width=\"16\" height=\"16\" aria-hidden=\"true\"><path d=\"m7.775 3.275 1.25-1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 4.95 4.95l-2.5 2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1-4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1 .018-1.042.751.751 0 0 1 1.042-.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l2.5-2.5a2.002 2.002 0 0 0-2.83-2.83l-1.25 1.25a.751.751 0 0 1-1.042-.018.751.751 0 0 1-.018-1.042Zm-4.69 9.64a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 2.83 0l1.25-1.25a.751.751 0 0 1 1.042.018.751.751 0 0 1 .018 1.042l-1.25 1.25a3.5 3.5 0 1 1-4.95-4.95l2.5-2.5a3.5 3.5 0 0 1 4.95 0 .751.751 0 0 1-.018 1.042.751.751 0 0 1-1.042.018 1.998 1.998 0 0 0-2.83 0l-2.5 2.5a1.998 1.998 0 0 0 0 2.83Z\"></path></svg></a></div>\n<ol dir=\"auto\">\n<li>Drop the plugin folder into <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/community\"><code>plugins/community/</code></a> (third-party plugins), or — to ship it bundled with Open Design — into the matching tier of <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/_official\"><code>plugins/_official/</code></a>.</li>\n<li>Pass validation: <code>od plugin validate</code>, <code>pnpm guard</code>, <code>pnpm --filter @open-design/plugin-runtime typecheck</code>.</li>\n<li>Fill the PR using the template in <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/spec/CONTRIBUTING.md\"><code>plugins/spec/CONTRIBUTING.md</code></a> (ID, version, lane, mode, capabilities, trigger examples; attach a screenshot / preview for visual plugins).</li>\n<li>To publish to an external registry (skills.sh / ClawHub / standalone GitHub) → <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/spec/PUBLISHING-REGISTRIES.md\"><code>plugins/spec/PUBLISHING-REGISTRIES.md</code></a>.</li>\n</ol>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Plugin registry endpoint: <code>GET /api/plugins</code>. Directory overview → <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/README.md\"><code>plugins/README.md</code></a> (<a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/README.zh-CN.md\">简体中文</a>).</p>\n<hr>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h2 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" </div></div>\n<markdown-accessiblity-table><table>\n<thead>\n<tr>\n<th>Layer</th>\n<th>Stack</th>\n</tr>\n</thead>\n<tbody>\n<tr>\n<td>Frontend</td>\n<td>Next.js 16 App Router + React 18 + TypeScript</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td>Daemon</td>\n<td>Node 24 · Express · SSE streaming · <code>better-sqlite3</code></td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td>Storage</td>\n<td>Files at <code>.od/projects/&lt;id&gt;/</code> + SQLite at <code>.od/app.sqlite</code> + <code>media-config.json</code> (gitignored, auto-created). <code>OD_DATA_DIR</code> relocates everything.</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td>Preview</td>\n<td>Sandboxed <code>srcdoc</code> iframe + streaming <code>&lt;artifact&gt;</code> parser</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td>Export</td>\n<td>HTML (inlined) · PDF (browser print) · PPTX (agent-driven) · ZIP · Markdown · MP4 (HyperFrames)</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td>Desktop</td>\n<td>Electron shell + sandboxed renderer + sidecar IPC (STATUS · EVAL · SCREENSHOT · CONSOLE · CLICK · SHUTDOWN)</td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td>Lifecycle</td>\n<td>One entry point: <code>pnpm tools-dev</code> (start / stop / run / status / logs / inspect / check)</td>\n</tr>\n</tbody>\n</table></markdown-accessiblity-table>\n<p dir=\"auto\">Full architecture → <a Many of the most-used skills, design systems, and plugins were written by people outside the core team.</p>\n<div class=\"markdown-heading\" dir=\"auto\"><h3 tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" <strong>skill</strong></td>\n<td>Drop a folder with <code>SKILL.md</code> + <code>assets/</code> + <code>references/</code></td>\n<td><a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/skills\"><code>skills/</code></a> · spec in <a <code>DESIGN.md</code> using the 9-section schema</td>\n<td><a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/design-systems\"><code>design-systems/&lt;brand&gt;/</code></a></td>\n</tr>\n<tr>\n<td>A new <strong>plugin</strong></td>\n<td>Drop <code>open-design.json</code> + manifest under a category folder</td>\n<td><a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/community\"><code>plugins/community/</code></a> · spec in <a href=\"/nexu-io/open-design/blob/main/plugins/spec/SPEC.md\"><code>plugins/spec/SPEC.md</code></a> · agent dev guide in <a CLI</strong></td>\n<td>One adapter entry + stream parser</td>\n<td><a tabindex=\"-1\" class=\"heading-element\" dir=\"auto\">🤖 Contributing as an agent</h3><a id=\"user-content--contributing-as-an-agent\" class=\"anchor\" aria-label=\"Permalink: 🤖 Contributing as an reading this</em>, the fastest path is:</p>\n<div class=\"highlight highlight-source-shell notranslate position-relative overflow-auto\" dir=\"auto\"><pre><span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> 1. Boot locally</span>\ngit clone https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design.git\n<span class=\"pl-c1\">cd</span> open-design <span class=\"pl-k\">&amp;&amp;</span> corepack <span class=\"pl-c1\">enable</span> <span class=\"pl-k\">&amp;&amp;</span> pnpm install\npnpm tools-dev run web\n\n<span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> 2. Find a good-first-issue and assign yourself</span>\ngh issue list --label <span class=\"pl-s\"><span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span>good first issue<span class=\"pl-pds\">\"</span></span> --state open --limit 20\ngh issue develop <span class=\"pl-k\">&lt;</span>number<span class=\"pl-k\">&gt;</span>   <span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> create a branch and worktree</span>\n\n<span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> 3. Make the change, run the checks</span>\npnpm guard <span class=\"pl-k\">&amp;&amp;</span> pnpm typecheck\npnpm --filter @open-design/<span class=\"pl-k\">&lt;</span>package<span class=\"pl-k\">&gt;</span> <span class=\"pl-c1\">test</span>\n\n<span class=\"pl-c\"><span class=\"pl-c\">#</span> 4. Open the PR</span>\ngh pr create --fill</pre><div class=\"zeroclipboard-container\">\n    <clipboard-copy aria-label=\"Copy code to clipboard\" class=\"ClipboardButton btn btn-invisible js-clipboard-copy m-2 p-0 d-flex flex-justify-center flex-items-center\" data-copy-feedback=\"Copied!\" data-tooltip-direction=\"w\" value=\"# 1.
